repositories.brief_summary: A quiet week focused on stdlib improvements, compiler internals refactoring, and cross-platform bug fixes in the Unix library and Windows runtime.
repositories.new_features_summary: Seq.repeat was made constant-space using a single cyclic closure/cons-cell pair, eliminating per-element allocation.
repositories.new_features: - [#14783](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14783) by [Jeremy Yallop](https://github.com/yallop): **`Seq.repeat` is now constant-space**, using a single cyclic closure and cons cell rather than allocating new cells for every element, dramatically reducing memory usage for long sequences.
repositories.activity_summary: Six PRs were merged into trunk covering stdlib, typing, libunix thread-safety, Windows error mapping, and CI hygiene.
repositories.activity:
- [#14800](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14800) by [Matthew Marques](https://github.com/mattm3465): **Localized `nondep_type_rec` memoisation state** in a new `Nondep_scope` module inside `typing/ctype.ml`, removing module-global hash tables and threading per-call scope instances through the recursion, reviewed by [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel).
- [#14715](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14715) by [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA): **Fixed several bugs in POSIX thread-safe functions in libunix** (`getgrnam_r`, `getgrgid_r`, `getpwnam_r`, `getpwuid_r`, `getlogin_r`), including null-pointer dereferences, a missing `HAVE_GETLOGIN_R` define in `caml/s.h.in`, and a typo `getgrid` → `getgrgid`; cherry-picked to 5.5 by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron).
- [#14795](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14795) by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron): **Removed an unnecessary mutual recursion** in `typing/env.ml` that had been dead since OCaml 4.00, splitting the `store_{type,value,...}` / `module_components` cluster into independent definitions.
- [#14794](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14794) by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron): **Fixed `ocamldoc` `is_function` predicate** to recognise module-dependent (modular explicit) function types, merged same day.
- [#14788](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14788) by [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb): **Mapped Win32 `ERROR_DIRECTORY` (267) to `ENOTDIR`** instead of `EUNKNOWNERR(-267)` so that e.g. `Unix.opendir` on a file raises the expected error on Windows; fixes [#14767](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14767).
- [#14787](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14787) by [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole): **Fixed a wrong variable name in GitHub Actions** (`GITHUB_TOKEN` rename not propagated to `check-changes-updated.sh`) and clarified CI comments introduced in [#14676](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14676).
- [#14676](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14676) by [Austin Theriault](https://github.com/ajbt200128): **Security fix preventing shell injection** in GitHub Actions workflows by placing GHA expression variables into environment variables rather than expanding them directly in `run` scripts.
repositories.discussions_summary: Active discussions covered runtime event buffer flooding, Dynarray concurrency contracts, and a stdlib proposal for a non-empty list type.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [#14791](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14791) opened by [nicki](https://github.com/curche): Proposes emitting **`EV_DOMAIN_CONDITION_WAIT` only in the instrumented runtime** after observing the event flooding ring buffers in large Infer runs (46K events vs. ~750 for other phases at `-j14`); [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) questioned whether the event should simply be removed, noting that `Condition.wait` calls are inherently a user-level action rather than a runtime action.
- [#14798](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14798) by [Nathan Taylor](https://github.com/dijkstracula): Proposes **rewording the `Dynarray` concurrency documentation** to be more precise; [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) confirmed that only resizing operations are truly unsafe, though noted the project has not yet done the broader work of precisely documenting concurrent usage for all stdlib mutable structures.
- [#14261](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/discussions/14261) (discussion by [Mathieu Barbin](https://github.com/mbarbin)): Ongoing conversation about **adding a `Nonempty_list.t` type to the stdlib** (using the `(::)` constructor trick for list-syntax compatibility) purely for interoperability between third-party libraries, without adding any API beyond the type definition.
repositories.trends_summary: The week shows continued investment in removing global mutable state from the typechecker and strengthening cross-platform correctness in the Unix library.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Elimination of global state in the typechecker**: [#14800](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14800) (`Nondep_scope`) continues a pattern of converting module-level hash tables into locally-scoped values, improving thread-safety and testability of internal compiler passes.
- **Cross-platform Unix correctness**: Merges of [#14715](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14715) and [#14788](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14788) both address platform-specific bugs in the Unix library, reflecting sustained effort to make OCaml's POSIX bindings correct on Linux, macOS, and Windows simultaneously.
- **Draft work on new GC pacing**: [#14796](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14796) by [Damien Doligez](https://github.com/damiendoligez) introduces a new GC pacing algorithm (686 additions across 17 files), an early-stage draft signalling future changes to GC throughput tuning.
- **Native atomic fetch-add across all backends**: [#14575](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14575) by [Zane Hambly](https://github.com/Zaneham) continues review for emitting native `Iatomic_fetch_add` on amd64, arm64, riscv, and power, advancing the multicore performance story.
repositories.contributors_summary: Active contributors this week included compiler regulars from Inria and the broader OCaml community working on typing, stdlib, and platform support.
repositories.contributors:
- [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) merged two PRs ([#14795](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14795), [#14794](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14794)) and co-reviewed [#14715](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14715).
- [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) landed the libunix thread-safety fixes in [#14715](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14715) and has [#14786](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14786) (GHA dependency updates) pending.
- [Matthew Marques](https://github.com/mattm3465) made his first contribution with [#14800](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14800), a clean refactoring of global state in `ctype.ml`.
- [Jeremy Yallop](https://github.com/yallop) contributed the constant-space `Seq.repeat` optimisation in [#14783](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14783).
- [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) fixed Windows error mapping in [#14788](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14788) and is reviewing [#14797](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14797).
- [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) reviewed and approved [#14800](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14800) and engaged in discussions on [#14791](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14791) and [#14798](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14798).
- [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) landed the CI variable-name fix in [#14787](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14787) and is reviewing [#14575](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14575).
- [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) provided detailed code review for [#14800](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14800), suggesting improved naming conventions for the new `Nondep_scope` API.

repositories.brief_summary: opam update gains file-to-directory rename support (closing a 7-year-old bug), with new open PRs on autopin cleanup UX and repository loading consistency.
repositories.new_features_summary: opam update now handles file-to-directory renames in repositories; depopts filter note made bold in Manual.
repositories.new_features:
- **`opam update` now handles file-to-directory and directory-to-file renames** in repositories, fixing a long-standing failure (first reported in 2019) where `opam update` would crash with a patch error when a repository changed a file into a directory of the same name or vice versa. The `OpamRepositoryBackend.get_diff` function was rewritten to abstract over repository layout types and the patch application logic was updated accordingly ([#6915](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6915), fixes [#3830](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/3830)).
- **New `OpamFilename.Unix` module** added to `src/core/opamFilename.ml`, abstracting over `/`-separated paths regardless of the host system. This underpins the cross-platform diff and patch work required by the repository layer overhaul ([#6915](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6915), also referenced as `#6914` in the changelog).
- **Improved visibility of the `depopts` filter note** in `doc/pages/Manual.md`: the note explaining the difference between `depopts` version filters and `conflicts:` is now rendered in bold, making the distinction easier to spot ([#6920](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6920), fixes [#5367](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/5367)).
repositories.activity_summary: New open PRs target autopin cleanup UX, repository loading consistency, and zsh completion; CI improvements merged for doc-triggered workflows.
repositories.activity:
- **Newly opened draft PR** by [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) ([#6923](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6923)) proposes that when `opam install` is aborted before confirming package installation, any automatically pinned packages are silently unpinned, complementing the regression test added in [#6922](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6922). Active review discussion with [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) is shaping the exact scope (abort vs. install failure).
- **Draft PR** by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) ([#6924](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6924)) works to homogenize initial repository loads and subsequent diff-based loads, enforcing that `load from diff` only looks under `packages/` and properly handles `files/` subdirectories. This is an alternative approach to the earlier [#6871](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6871) (still open as a draft by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou)) and is part of the larger repository tar.gz archive story ([#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625)).
- **Draft PR** by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) ([#6929](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6929)) extracts the patch file rename support from [#6915](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6915) for further review and extension.
- **Open PR** by [Talel Zighni](https://github.com/ta2005) ([#6930](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6930)) adds `return 0` to the zsh completion script to prevent opam-related error codes from leaking into the shell prompt.
- **Open PR** by [Guilherme FACCINI BANDEIRA](https://github.com/faccinib) ([#6921](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6921)) standardizes bold/italic usage for environment variables, options, and placeholder arguments in opam manpages, with ongoing discussion involving [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) on cmdliner manpage conventions.
- **Open PR** by [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) ([#6910](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6910)) removes unnecessary `chdir` calls (porting work from [#5966](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/5966)), which also surfaces the `opam pin list` crash seen in the aborted-install test. A newly found bug around `of_basename` and absolute paths is noted for follow-up.
- **Merged:** regression test for aborted local install leaving packages half-pinned by [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) ([#6922](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6922)), documenting the current crash in `opam pin list` as a baseline for the forthcoming fix.
- **Merged:** fix for `opam update` on file/directory renames by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) ([#6915](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6915)), closing [#3830](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/3830).
- **Merged:** documentation improvement for `depopts` filter note by [Corentin Coulombel](https://github.com/ccoulombel) ([#6920](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6920)), closing [#5367](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/5367).
- **CI improvement** by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) ([#6927](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6927)) ensures that changes under `doc/` now trigger the documentation build job in GitHub Actions, fixing [#6810](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6810). A follow-up issue ([#6926](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6926)) tracks splitting `main.yml` into separate workflow files for finer-grained triggering.
repositories.discussions_summary: Manpage formatting conventions debated in #6921; autopin cleanup UX explored in #6923; absolute path bug filed in #6925.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- Discussion in [#6921](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6921) between [Guilherme FACCINI BANDEIRA](https://github.com/faccinib), [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate), and [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) clarified that manpage conventions call for **bold** (`$(b,...)`) for literal text such as environment variable names and options, and **italic/underline** (`$(i,...)`) for replaceable argument placeholders — aligning with the Linux man-pages conventions referenced by cmdliner.
- In [#6923](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6923), [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) and [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) discussed the right UX boundary: the agreed approach is to silently **unpin automatically pinned packages when the user declines installation** (i.e. answers "no" at the confirmation prompt), while leaving packages pinned after an install *failure* since the user's intent was to install them and partial state may have already been written.
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) filed [#6925](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6925) noting that `opam config subst` raises a fatal `File_not_found` when given an **absolute pathname**, because it unconditionally prepends `$PWD`. Discovered while reviewing [#6910](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6910).
- Issue [#6890](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6890) tracking intermittent Windows CI failures in `legacy-local` received an additional trace from [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate), pointing to `OpamSystem.File_not_found` on a switch-state file and suggesting that `caml_win32_rename` may not be atomic in GHA's NTFS environment.
repositories.trends_summary: Work continues on repository layer modernisation (tar.gz archive support story) and improving Windows/CI reliability.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The **repository layer overhaul** (tracking issue [#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625)) is generating sustained activity: [#6915](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6915) rewrote `get_diff`, [#6924](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6924) and [#6871](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6871) tackle `load_from_diff` consistency, and [#6929](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6929) continues extracting patch-file rename support — all preparatory work for supporting tar.gz archives as an internal repository representation.
- **Windows CI flakiness** in `legacy-local` ([#6890](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6890)) is a recurring backdrop: multiple PRs this week note that Windows failures are being deliberately ignored, suggesting this is an open reliability concern rather than a resolved issue.
- **Local install UX** (aborted installs leaving packages half-pinned) is being addressed systematically: a regression test landed in [#6922](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6922), the `chdir` removal in [#6910](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6910) unblocks `opam pin list`, and the cleanup prompt is being designed in [#6923](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6923).
repositories.contributors_summary: Five contributors active this week across code, reviews, documentation, and issue triage.
repositories.contributors:
- [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) authored the core implementation in [#6915](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6915) (rewriting `OpamRepositoryBackend.get_diff`, adding `OpamFilename.Unix`, updating the patch test suite), reviewed by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate), and is continuing related repository work in the open draft [#6871](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6871).
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) reviewed and merged [#6915](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6915), [#6922](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6922), and [#6920](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6920); opened [#6924](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6924) and [#6929](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6929) as draft PRs; filed issues [#6925](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6925) and [#6926](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6926); and landed the CI doc-trigger fix [#6927](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6927).
- [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) merged [#6922](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6922) (regression test for aborted install) and opened the autopin cleanup draft [#6923](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6923), with ongoing work on [#6910](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6910) (chdir removal).
- [Corentin Coulombel](https://github.com/ccoulombel) contributed the documentation fix [#6920](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6920), improving the visibility of the `depopts` filter note in the opam Manual.
- [Guilherme FACCINI BANDEIRA](https://github.com/faccinib) opened [#6921](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6921) to standardize manpage formatting of options and environment variables, engaging in discussion with [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) and [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) to refine the approach.
- [Talel Zighni](https://github.com/ta2005) opened [#6930](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6930) to fix zsh completion leaking error codes into the shell prompt.

repositories.brief_summary: Merlin compatibility fix and improved developer setup via opam pin-depends in ocaml-lsp-server.opam.template.
repositories.new_features_summary: Merlin module rename compat fix and opam pin-depends for frictionless local dev setup.
repositories.new_features:
- Updated `merlin_config.ml` to use `Ocaml_utils.Misc` instead of the now-removed `Merlin_utils.Misc`, tracking upstream [ocaml/merlin#2059](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2059) ([#1601](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1601))
- Added `pin-depends` for `merlin-lib` and `ocaml-index` directly in `ocaml-lsp-server.opam.template`, so `opam switch create .` now works without manually pinning a Merlin development branch ([#1603](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1603))
- Removed redundant CI steps that manually pinned Merlin, as the pin is now declared in the opam metadata ([#1603](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1603))
repositories.activity_summary: Automated flake.lock update PR remains open; opam downgrade issue on OCaml 4.14 was reported.
repositories.activity:
- The automated **flake.lock update** PR ([#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1437)) remains open, bumping `merlin5_3` and `nixpkgs` inputs to May 2026 revisions
- [Török Edwin](https://github.com/edwintorok) opened [#1604](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1604) reporting that `opam upgrade` on OCaml 4.14 attempts to downgrade `ocaml-lsp-server` from `1.21.0-4.14` to `1.17.0` due to version resolution interplay between the `lsp` sub-library split
- [Illia Denysenko](https://github.com/juliancoffee) opened [#1602](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1602) after hitting a build failure (`Unbound module Ocaml_utils.Misc`) when following the README setup instructions; this was resolved by PR [#1603](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1603) and the issue was closed
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) merged the **Merlin compatibility fix** PR ([#1601](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1601)) adapting to a module renaming in upstream Merlin
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion on issue #1602 walked through the opam template approach; issue #1604 raised a version resolution concern on OCaml 4.14.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- On [#1602](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1602), [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) explained how to use `ocaml-lsp-server.opam.template` with `pin-depends` to replace the fragile CI-only Merlin pin, and [Illia Denysenko](https://github.com/juliancoffee) implemented the fix as PR [#1603](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1603)
- On [#1604](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1604), [Török Edwin](https://github.com/edwintorok) described how opam resolves `lsp >= 1.22` as unavailable on OCaml 4.14, leading to an unexpected downgrade; a suggested workaround of restricting the `lsp` package's OCaml version availability was noted as potentially the right fix
repositories.trends_summary: Active upstream Merlin tracking and developer onboarding friction being addressed as recurring themes.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Ongoing **tight coupling with upstream Merlin development**: the need for both a compatibility patch ([#1601](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1601)) and an opam pin ([#1603](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1603)) in the same week reflects that ocaml-lsp tracks Merlin's `main` branch closely between releases
- **Developer onboarding friction** surfacing through issues: [#1602](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1602) and [#1604](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1604) both stem from opam package metadata not accurately reflecting build-time requirements, pointing to a recurring gap between the release cycle of ocaml-lsp and its Merlin dependency
repositories.contributors_summary: Two contributors merged PRs; one reporter opened a packaging issue that remains open.
repositories.contributors:
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) merged the Merlin compatibility fix ([#1601](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1601)), reviewed and merged [Illia Denysenko](https://github.com/juliancoffee)'s opam template PR ([#1603](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1603)), and provided detailed guidance in the [#1602](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1602) discussion on using `pin-depends` in opam templates
- [Illia Denysenko](https://github.com/juliancoffee) reported the build failure in [#1602](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1602) and contributed the fix as PR [#1603](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1603), including the opam template, promoted opam file, and CI cleanup
- [Török Edwin](https://github.com/edwintorok) opened [#1604](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1604) with a detailed analysis of the opam version resolution problem affecting OCaml 4.14 users after an `opam upgrade`

repositories.brief_summary: Merged misc.ml compiler sync; new ocamlgrep structural search PR opened for integration discussion.
repositories.new_features_summary: Internal refactor moves Misc module to ocaml_utils to reduce compiler diff.
repositories.new_features:
- The `Misc` module relocated from `merlin_utils` to `ocaml_utils` (`src/ocaml/utils/misc.ml`), reducing the diff between Merlin's internal copy and the upstream compiler's version ([#2059](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2059)). This is an internal refactoring that improves long-term maintainability by placing shared compiler-derived modules under `src/ocaml/`.
- Also moved `Format_doc` to `src/ocaml/utils/format_doc.ml(i)` as part of the same cleanup, eliminating a historical placement that had been used to avoid a dependency cycle ([#2059](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2059)).
repositories.activity_summary: New structural OCaml code search PR opened; misc.ml compiler sync merged; saved-parts testing PR ongoing.
repositories.activity:
- [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) opened [#2064](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2064) proposing integration of **ocamlgrep** (structural, type-aware search of OCaml typed trees) as a new `ocamlgrep` subcommand in merlin. The PR adds a new `src/project` library that uses `dune describe workspace` to enumerate cmt files, and an `Expr_search` module for pattern matching against typed trees. Still open and actively being refined.
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923)'s **misc.ml compiler synchronization** ([#2059](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2059)) was merged, restructuring files under `src/ocaml/utils/` and manually reconciling accumulated drift between Merlin and the compiler's `utils/misc.ml`.
- [Thomas Refis](https://github.com/trefis)'s **saved-parts testing** PR ([#2060](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2060)) remains open, investigating a subtle difference in how Merlin handles saved types compared to the upstream typechecker, motivated by review of [ocaml/ocaml#14241](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14241).
repositories.discussions_summary: Active discussion on how best to integrate ocamlgrep into Merlin, covering architecture, AI tooling policy, and scope.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- Issue [#2063](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2063) sparked a detailed architectural discussion initiated by [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) about integrating ocamlgrep into Merlin. [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) raised concerns about the current PR's dependency structure and suggested that a deeper integration — sharing Merlin's artifact caches and configuration for project root discovery — would be more valuable than a standalone wrapper. He also flagged a potential tension with the existing **search-by-type** feature and its different query language.
- [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) supported the integration direction in [#2063](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2063) and proposed scheduling a synchronization meeting to align on technical choices.
- [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) raised the question of AI-assisted contributions in [#2063](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2063), suggesting that maintainers might prefer receiving detailed design proposals over large AI-generated PRs, and offered to reduce [#2064](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2064) to a pure issue if that better fits the project's workflow.
- [Pierre R.](https://github.com/gr-im) noted in [#2064](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2064) that the current dependency lattice in the ocamlgrep PR seemed inverted compared to expected layering, with ocamlgrep ideally exposing an API under `merlin-lib` rather than the other way around.
repositories.trends_summary: Growing interest in type-aware structural search; continued effort to reduce diff with upstream compiler.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Type-aware code search** is gaining traction as a use case for Merlin's infrastructure: both the new ocamlgrep PR ([#2064](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2064)) and the existing search-by-type feature represent overlapping interests that the maintainers are now actively discussing how to reconcile.
- A recurring theme of **reducing drift from the upstream OCaml compiler** continues, with the misc.ml synchronization ([#2059](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2059)) being the latest step in a broader effort to keep Merlin's copies of compiler modules in sync.
- The project is beginning to articulate a position on **AI-assisted contributions**: the discussion in [#2063](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2063) surfaced that guidelines are being drafted, with an expectation that authors must have complete ownership and understanding of AI-generated changes.
repositories.contributors_summary: Liam Stevenson merged misc.ml sync; Martin Jambon opened ocamlgrep PR and issue; Thomas Refis, Ulysse, Nicolás Ojeda Bär, and Pierre R. contributed reviews and discussion.
repositories.contributors:
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) authored the **misc.ml synchronization** ([#2059](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2059)), which was merged this week. The work involved manually reconciling accumulated differences across 33 files between Merlin and the compiler.
- [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) opened the **ocamlgrep integration** PR ([#2064](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2064)) and the corresponding tracking issue ([#2063](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2063)). He is also actively self-reviewing [#2064](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2064), posting detailed inline comments identifying areas for improvement.

- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) provided substantive review feedback on the integration approach in [#2063](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2063), outlining architectural concerns and suggesting the direction for a deeper integration with Merlin's caching infrastructure.

repositories.brief_summary: Week 19 of 2026 was a highly active week for ocaml/dune, dominated by the formal release of **dune 3.23.0** on 2026-05-05 and immediate follow-on work to address regressions in a 3.23.1 release candidate. Major themes included: sandbox and build-engine hardening (live-sandbox throttling, pending-target lifecycle refactoring, file-descriptor throttling), a significant package management milestone (relocatable OCaml compiler support merged), opam generation bug fixes gated on `(lang dune 3.23)`, ongoing melange test coverage expansion, RPC infrastructure improvements (flush-file-watcher command, RPC status in status line, debug dumps), and a large ongoing effort to implement per-module inter-library dependency filtering ([#14472](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14472)) that aims to dramatically reduce unnecessary incremental recompilation in large monorepos.
repositories.new_features_summary: New features this week were anchored in the dune 3.23.0 release changelog and new PRs merged to main. Key additions include: the relocatable OCaml compiler being used by default in package management, a new `dune rpc flush-file-watcher` RPC procedure, composable status-line sections for RPC connection display, a global sandbox throttle preventing unbounded sandbox directory creation, and array-backed filename sets for more compact in-memory representations during watch mode.
repositories.new_features:
- **Relocatable compiler support in `dune pkg`** ([#14357](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14357)): Dune now detects and uses the relocatable OCaml compiler from David Allsopp's opam repository when available, enabling the compiler to be treated as a cacheable package rather than requiring constant toolchain rebuilds. This closes long-standing issue [#13229](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13229) and [#14012](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14012).
- **`dune rpc flush-file-watcher` command** ([#14467](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14467)): A new RPC procedure `flush_file_watcher` (returning `[ \`Ok | \`Not_in_watch_mode ]`) was added to `dune-rpc`'s public API, exposed as `dune rpc flush-file-watcher`. This allows RPC clients to synchronise with the file watcher before issuing build requests.
- **RPC connection status in forwarded builds** ([#14424](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14424)): The build status line now shows a composable `[rpc N]` section indicating the number of active RPC clients. The `Console.Status_line` module gained `add_section` and `remove_section` APIs to support persistent composable status-line fragments.
- **Array-backed filename sets in load-rules** ([#14487](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14487)): Long-lived filename sets in watch mode are now backed by sorted arrays (`Filename.Array.Set`) rather than balanced trees. The `Filename_set`, `load_rules`, `build_config`, `fs_memo`, and `dune_project` APIs were updated to use `Filename.Array.Set.t` instead of `Filename.Set.t`, reducing memory footprint in long-running watch sessions.
- **Live sandbox throttle** ([#14464](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14464)): A global throttle of 250 slots now limits concurrently live sandbox directories. Previously, all ready sandboxed rules could pre-create their directories before any action ran, causing unbounded disk usage.
- **Sandbox lifecycle refactored into `Sandbox.with_`** ([#14483](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14483)): `Sandbox.create`/`Sandbox.destroy` have been replaced by a single `Sandbox.with_` that accepts an optional sandbox mode. A `None` mode creates a lightweight fake context for non-sandboxed actions that only tracks pending targets, and is not affected by the live-sandbox throttle.
- **Removal of `.all-deps` transitive dependency files** ([#14407](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14407)): Transitive module dependency closures are now computed directly from anonymous `ocamldep` and `ocamlobjinfo` actions rather than materialising `.all-deps` intermediate files. The `Filename.Extension.all_deps` symbol was removed. This also fixes an ugly "Rule not found" error.
- **`dune install` now raises a user error when package management is active** ([#14427](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14427)): Running `dune install` in a workspace with Dune package management enabled now produces a clean error message rather than crashing or silently misbehaving.
- **`cram`: boolean expressions in `runtest_alias`** ([#14425](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14425)): Cram tests now support boolean expressions in the `runtest_alias` field.
- **Dune 3.23.0 released** ([#14212](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14212)): The release shipped on 2026-05-05 with many features including user-rule sandboxing by default, `compile_commands.json` generation for C/C++ stubs, shared jsoo runtimes, hardlink sandboxing on Windows, `dune clean` accepting target arguments, and numerous bug fixes.
repositories.activity_summary: The week saw 50+ commits to `main`, a formal 3.23.0 release, and active work on a 3.23.1 release candidate addressing post-release regressions. Build-engine hardening (sandbox lifecycle, file descriptor throttling, array-backed data structures) dominated the merged work alongside opam generation fixes, package management advances, and melange test coverage.
repositories.activity:
- **Dune 3.23.0 released** ([#14212](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14212)): [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) cut the release on 2026-05-05, kicking off the 3.23.1 regression-fix cycle tracked in [#14441](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14441).
- **Fix opam dune-bound deduplication gating** ([#14466](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14466), merged): [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) gated the dune-version-bound deduplication (introduced in 3.23.0) on `(lang dune 3.23)`, fixing [#14436](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14436) where the opam generation changed for projects still on older `dune-project` lang versions.
- **Fix menhir opam dep injection** ([#14434](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14434), merged): Only inject `menhir >= 20180523` for packages that actually use menhir, fixing [#14428](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14428).
- **Fix support for building dune on older OCaml switches** ([#14443](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14443), merged): [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) fixed a regression that broke bootstrapping dune with `ocamlfind-secondary` on OCaml < 4.14.
- **Resolve directory symlinks in fetched package targets** ([#13792](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13792), merged): [Ambre Austen Suhamy](https://github.com/ElectreAAS) landed support for resolving directory symlinks in fetched source archives (e.g. ocamlbuild), partially fixing [#9873](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/9873).
- **Fix topological lib order in shared jsoo runtime** ([#14438](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14438), merged): [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) fixed a bug where alphabetical sorting of the runtime key broke the ordering of JS files in shared runtimes, which would silently change which conflicting binding wins.
- **Unvendor blake3 mini** ([#14462](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14462), merged): The vendored blake3 mini library was removed from the dune tree, and blake3 tests were added to `test/expect-tests/`.
- **Add active RPC requests to debug dump** ([#14405](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14405), merged): [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) added in-flight RPC request tracking and included it in the SIGUSR2 debug event dump.
- **Backport #14466 to 3.23.1-rc** ([#14471](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14471), merged): Bit-for-bit identical backport of the opam generation fix.
- **Backport #14443 to 3.23.1-rc** ([#14465](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14465), merged): Backport of the ocamlfind-secondary fix.
- **Refactor: use `Repr.t` for persistent descriptors** ([#14470](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14470), merged): Persistent file descriptors now carry a `Repr.t` for generic debug rendering.
- **Move build handling into the engine** ([#14459](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14459), merged): Continued engine refactoring by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg).
- **Make cache repro check deterministic** ([#14468](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14468), merged).
- **Melange test coverage expansion**: [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) merged several melange test PRs: [#14418](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14418) (nested `include_subdirs qualified`), [#14419](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14419) (`include_subdirs unqualified` + `wrapped false`), [#14420](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14420) (wrapped library paths), [#14478](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14478) (melange-specific source preference), [#14480](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14480) (melange sources in public libs), and exposed `Lib_dep`/`Lib_mode` functions ([#14475](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14475)).
- **Lock dir odoc check** ([#14426](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14426), merged): Fix to check the lock dir even when `lock_dev_tools` is disabled when invoking odoc.
- **Test for menhir missing dep for inferred mli** ([#14447](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14447), merged): Reproduction test for the race condition reported in [#14450](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14450).
- **Various regression guard tests** merged by [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb): [#14439](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14439) (root_module + libraries), [#14440](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14440) (ppx_runtime_libraries cmi-dep coverage).
- **`bin pform` test reorganisation** ([#14431](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14431), merged): [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) organised all `%{bin:...}` tests into a dedicated `test/blackbox-tests/test-cases/bin-pform/` directory in preparation for [#14432](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14432).
repositories.discussions_summary: The most significant discussions centred on the 3.23.1 regression cycle, a long-running debate about per-module dependency filtering performance trade-offs, the `dune install` and package management compatibility question, and the portable lock directory single-solve effort.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **3.23.1 regression tracker** ([#14441](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14441)): [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) opened a dedicated tracker immediately after the 3.23.0 release. Regressions being backported include: menhir auto-inject affecting all packages ([#14428](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14428), fixed by [#14434](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14434)), opam bound deduplication activating for pre-3.23 projects ([#14436](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14436), fixed by [#14466](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14466)), OCaml < 4.14 build breakage ([#14443](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14443)), and the jsoo shared runtime ordering bug ([#14438](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14438)). [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) and [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) noted that [#14438](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14438) (jsoo runtime) is itself a regression from 3.23.0, introduced by [#13621](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13621).
- **Per-module inter-library dependency filtering** ([#14472](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14472)): [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) opened a restructured version of the long-running [#14116](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14116) PR addressing [#4572](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/4572). [Alain Frisch](https://github.com/alainfrisch) engaged in a detailed discussion about the trade-off between per-module fine-grained deps (larger graph, less recompilation) vs. a two-layered lib+module representation or a compiler-side `.cmx` stability check. The consensus was that graph pruning is the right approach but the null-build overhead needs measurement.
- **`dune install` and package management** ([#14449](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14449)): [Sudha Parimala](https://github.com/Sudha247) documented the current incompatibility, noting that `dune install` is used beyond opam (Arch Linux, Fedora, Debian, Nix packaging). The question of whether to support it in a package-management-enabled workspace was left open.
- **Opam generation stability** ([#14436](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14436)): Extended discussion between [Mathieu Barbin](https://github.com/mbarbin) and [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) about the expectation that `dune-project` lang version gates all changes to generated opam files. [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) confirmed this as the intended policy going forward and apologised for the missed gating in [#14175](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14175).
- **Portable lock directories single solve** ([#14458](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14458)): [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) opened a draft PR based on earlier work with [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) to implement a single SAT solve for all platforms, fixing [#13645](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13645).
- **Menhir missing dep for inferred .mli** ([#14450](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14450)): [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) reported a race condition where menhir-generated parsers sometimes fail with "inconsistent assumptions over interface" due to untracked dependencies in the `ocamlc -i` mock-compile step. [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) expressed interest in investigating as it relates to ongoing per-module filtering work.
- **`top-module` over-invalidation** ([#14477](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14477)): [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) filed a performance bug where `dune ocaml top-module` rebuilds artefacts when any `(libraries ...)` dep's interface changes, even libraries only referenced from `.mli` (which top-module drops). A reproduction test was opened in [#14476](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14476).
repositories.trends_summary: Several converging trends are visible: a sustained push to reduce unnecessary incremental recompilation through per-module dependency filtering; hardening of the build engine's resource management (sandbox throttling, file descriptor limits); growing melange integration test coverage; and a shift toward relocatable compiler support enabling better caching of toolchains in package management.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Per-module dependency precision**: The multi-month effort ([#14472](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14472), [#14473](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14473), [#14474](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14474)) by [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) to use `ocamldep` output to filter compile-rule dependencies per module — rather than globbing all libraries — continues to grow in scope, with [Alain Frisch](https://github.com/alainfrisch) engaging on performance implications for large monorepos.
- **Build engine resource management**: Multiple PRs this week ([#14464](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14464), [#14483](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14483), [#14462](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14462), [#14487](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14487), [#14460](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14460)) show a sustained drive by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) to reduce memory pressure, file descriptor exhaustion, and disk usage in long-running watch sessions.
- **Relocatable compiler integration**: The merge of [#14357](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14357) closes out the [#13229](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13229) tracker, but follow-on issues ([#14456](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14456), [#14457](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14457)) show work continuing on portable lock directories and reducing duplication in lock file structures.
- **Melange single-context universal**: Several melange PRs from [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) carry the `single-context-universal` label, indicating progress toward a unified melange/OCaml compilation context.
- **Post-release regression discipline**: The 3.23.1 release cycle demonstrates a matured practice of gating behaviour changes on `(lang dune X.Y)` version, with [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb)'s [#14466](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14466) and [#14453](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14453) both retroactively applying version guards to previously unguarded changes.
repositories.issues_summary: Several new issues were filed covering release regressions (menhir spurious deps, opam generation instability, OCaml < 4.14 support), new feature requests (NetBSD compilation fix, warning for dune files inside cram test directories, portable lock directory deduplication), and longstanding correctness bugs (menhir missing dep for inferred .mli, top-module over-invalidation).

repositories.brief_summary: Bug fixes for xref2 substitution failures in merlin-lib and core_unix, roadmap update, and OxCaml zero_alloc annotation PR in review.
repositories.new_features_summary: ROADMAP.md restructured with OxCaml support, new markup syntax, custom tags, improved source rendering, and Sherlodoc plans.
repositories.new_features:
- The **ROADMAP.md** was substantially restructured by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) to reflect current development priorities ([#1421](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1421)). The updated roadmap now details four key Odoc work streams: **OxCaml support** (targeting all relevant OxCaml compiler features including modes and layouts, tied to specific OxCaml releases), **new markup syntax** (admonitions, quotes, definition lists, and details), **custom tags and plugins** (extensible tag handling with a plugin mechanism), and **improved source rendering** with new syntax for linking to implementations from doc comments. For Sherlodoc, the roadmap now calls for markdown output support, search in documentation pages, and improved incremental building.
- A **sherlodoc test correction** fixed an incorrect argument name in the `link_in_docstring` cram test ([9695cd7](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/commit/9695cd79ec29c082f62c768a99a650ebf06892d3)).
repositories.activity_summary: Zero_alloc OxCaml annotation PR is under active review; bug fixes for xref2 substitution failures in merlin-lib and core_unix landed just after the week.
repositories.activity:
- The **`[@@zero_alloc]` annotation for OxCaml** PR by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) ([#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)) is open and under active review. The PR parses `zero_alloc` attributes on signatures and retains them in the parsed output, supporting `opt`, `strict`, and `arity <n>` payloads. [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) provided detailed review feedback on the attribute type representation and payload handling.
- **Build failures** for merlin-lib ([#1429](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1429)) and core_unix ([#1431](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1431)) were reported by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) as regressions in the xref2 substitution path (`subst.ml` assertion failure and `apply_inner_substs` uncaught exception respectively). The fix PR [#1430](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1430) by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) merged just after the week (May 11).
- An **OxCaml opam conflict constraint** for versions older than 5.2.0minus31 was added by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) ([#1432](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1432)), ensuring compatibility with the OxCaml compiler version requirements; this also merged May 11.
- The **`bisect_ppx` dependency** was removed from odoc's opam file by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) ([#1428](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1428)), as it is not needed for tests and the coverage GHA workflow installs it separately.
- The ongoing **OxCaml support tracking issue** ([#1411](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1411)) by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) continues to coordinate work on modes, layouts, and other OxCaml-specific features across multiple sub-PRs.
repositories.discussions_summary: Active review discussion on zero_alloc annotation representation and handling of invalid payloads in PR #1422.
repositories.notable_discussions: - The **`[@@zero_alloc]` PR review** ([#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)) involved substantive discussion between [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) and [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) on the type representation of the annotation. Two options were considered: a record type `{ opt; strict; arity }` vs. a variant `Arity of n | Opt | Strict`. The discussion also covered whether the annotation form `val[@zero_alloc]` (inline) or `[@@zero_alloc]` (postfix) is more familiar to OxCaml users, and how to handle invalid payloads (silently ignore vs. emit a warning, given that the OxCaml compiler will catch errors anyway).
repositories.trends_summary: OxCaml integration is the dominant theme, with xref2 bug fixes arising from increased testing against real-world OCaml 5.x packages.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **OxCaml-specific feature support** is becoming a sustained focus, with the tracking issue ([#1411](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1411)), the open zero_alloc PR ([#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)), the new roadmap section, and the opam conflict fix ([#1432](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1432)) all reflecting coordinated effort to support OxCaml in odoc.
- **Real-world compatibility testing** against packages like `base`, `merlin-lib`, and `core_unix` is surfacing xref2 edge cases (issues [#1426](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1426), [#1429](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1429), [#1431](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1431)), indicating that odoc's xref2 substitution logic is being exercised by newer OCaml 5.x patterns and real package builds.
repositories.contributors_summary: Jon Ludlam drove bug fixes and maintenance; David Allsopp added OxCaml compatibility; Marek Kubica contributed the zero_alloc annotation PR with review from Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac.
repositories.contributors:
- [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) merged the fix for `base` build failures with OCaml 5.5 ([#1427](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1427)), removed the `bisect_ppx` dependency ([#1428](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1428)), updated ROADMAP.md ([#1421](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1421)), opened bug reports for merlin-lib ([#1429](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1429)) and core_unix ([#1431](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1431)) build failures, and opened the fix PR for those failures ([#1430](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1430)).
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) opened and merged the OxCaml opam conflict constraint PR ([#1432](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1432)) to ensure compatibility with OxCaml compiler version requirements.
- [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) opened the zero_alloc annotation PR ([#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)) adding `[@@zero_alloc]` support to odoc's OxCaml output, and iterated on the implementation based on review feedback.
- [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) provided detailed review on the zero_alloc PR ([#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)), including type representation guidance, payload validation discussion, and a bug attribution note for the xref2 fix ([#1427](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1427)).
- [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) maintains the OxCaml support tracking issue ([#1411](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1411)) coordinating the broader OxCaml feature work.

repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion on [#3641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3641) raised concerns about AI-generated release summaries and the process for obtaining release announcements from maintainers.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [#3641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3641) [Jules Aguillon](https://github.com/Julow) flagged that one code block in the draft mdx 2.5.2 changelog was malformed, and suggested removing an inaccurate section rather than keep an AI-generated summary that diverges from the actual changelog.

repositories.trends_summary: The week shows increased focus on filling historical gaps in platform release documentation and tightening contribution guidelines for community-submitted listings.
repositories.emerging_trends:

- Dune alpha release changelogs are now being tracked with `ignore: true` in the platform releases data ([#3640](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3640)), enabling newsletter generation from complete data while suppressing premature public announcements.
- Contribution guidelines ([#3645](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3645)) now explicitly address trademark and legal concerns for third-party industrial user submissions, reflecting increasing interest from companies in being listed on the site.

repositories.brief_summary: Archival of packages requiring OCaml < 4.11, dune 3.23.0 release, and a cluster of robur-coop/Miou ecosystem packages.
repositories.new_features_summary: OCaml compiler cutoff raised to 4.11; dune 3.23.0 adds sandboxed rules by default and compile_commands.json generation.
repositories.new_features:
- The **archiving policy cutoff** was raised from OCaml 4.08 to **4.11** in `governance/policies/archiving.md`, reflecting the merge of [#29848](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29848) which removed over 53,000 lines of package definitions for compilers below this threshold.
- **dune 3.23.0** was released ([#29844](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29844)): user rules are now **sandboxed by default**, `dune clean` accepts arguments to remove specific targets, `dune promote` promotes all paths matching a prefix, and `compile_commands.json` is generated for C/C++ foreign stubs when building `@check` (enabling clangd and similar tools). The minimum OCaml version to build dune itself was raised from 4.08 to 4.14.
- **`CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`** was formally added to the repository ([#29850](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29850)), officially adopting the [OCaml Code of Conduct](https://github.com/ocaml/code-of-conduct).
- **Camlp5 8.05.00** adds support for **OCaml 5.5.0 syntax changes** ([#29874](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29874)), and a follow-up bugfix release 8.04.01 corrects labeled tuple handling in constructor expressions ([#29884](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29884)).
- **miou 0.6.0** ([#29855](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29855)) adds a new `Miou.Trace` module with runtime event integration for tracing scheduler events, and `Miou.take` for consuming tasks from `Miou.orphans` with the ability to cancel rather than await them.
- **vif 0.0.1~beta3** ([#29876](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29876)) gains support for **UNIX domain sockets** as listeners, graceful server shutdown, an OPTIONS route method, and improved handling of large file transfers.
- **art 0.4.0** ([#29873](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29873)) adds the `Art.map` function to the Adaptive Radix Tree library.
- **ppx_deriving_jsonschema 0.0.7** ([#29819](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29819)) adds `[@@jsonschema.compact_variants]`, `[@@jsonschema.format]`, `[@@jsonschema.description]`, `[@jsonschema.default]`, and `[@jsonschema.attrs]` attributes, and makes `option` types nullable in generated schemas.
- **`conf-jq`** extended with OpenBSD package name ([#29871](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29871)) and **`conf-cmake`** extended with NixOS depext support ([#29856](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29856)).
- **`conf-guile`** corrected for OpenBSD (uses `databases/guile3`) and RHEL 9 (`guile30-devel`) ([#29872](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29872)).
repositories.activity_summary: Dominated by the OCaml 4.11 archival, dune 3.23.0, and a concentrated batch of robur-coop Miou-ecosystem releases.
repositories.activity:

- [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) released **dune 3.23.0** (18 packages) ([#29844](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29844)), superseding an earlier draft pre-release PR ([#29824](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29824)) that was closed to avoid CI overload.

- [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) released **mirage 4.11.0** ([#29870](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29870)), **ca-certs 1.0.3** ([#29869](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29869)), **ca-certs-nss 3.123.1** ([#29868](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29868)), and **albatross 2.7.1** ([#29851](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29851)).
- [@chetmurthy](https://github.com/chetmurthy) released **Camlp5 8.05.00** ([#29874](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29874)) and bugfix **8.04.01** ([#29884](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29884)); [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri) reviewed and adjusted the OCaml version upper bounds.
- [Reynir Björnsson](https://github.com/reynir) released **git-net and git-kv 0.2.2** ([#29877](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29877)), fixing timezone handling and avoiding float/int64 for timestamps.
- New standalone packages: **doctor 0.1.0** diagnostic CLI for OCaml environments by [@funwithcthulhu](https://github.com/funwithcthulhu) ([#29854](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29854), originally submitted as `ocaml-doctor` in [#29845](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29845) and renamed per naming policy); **ppx_forbid and ppx_enforce 0.1.0** compile-time module/function access control PPX tools by [@mathiasbourgoin](https://github.com/mathiasbourgoin) ([#29853](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29853)); and **sha256-cng 0.1.0** Windows-only SHA-256 via native CNG/BCrypt API by [elfy](https://github.com/elfyboo) ([#29885](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29885)).
- [Mathieu Barbin](https://github.com/mbarbin) released **cmdlang 0.0.11** (5 packages) ([#29878](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29878)), enabling OCaml 5.4/5.5 CI and deprecating `Param.create`; [@mathiasbourgoin](https://github.com/mathiasbourgoin) also released **miaou 0.5.2** (10 packages) ([#29862](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29862)).
- [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat) released **ocaml_intrinsics_kernel v0.17.2** ([#29880](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29880)) fixing MSVC compilation errors; [Frédéric Recoules](https://github.com/recoules) released **unisim_archisec 0.0.14** ([#29879](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29879)) fixing x86 instruction decoding segfaults.
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) released **opam-0install 0.6.0** ([#29847](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29847)), adding cmdliner 2.0.0+ compatibility.
- [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri) fixed the **ppx_deriving_jsonschema** revdep on **caisar** by adding an upper bound ([#29849](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29849)) after [José Nogueira](https://github.com/thatportugueseguy)'s [#29819](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29819) introduced a breaking API change.
- [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) extended depexts for **conf-jq** on OpenBSD ([#29871](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29871)) and fixed **conf-guile** port names on OpenBSD and RHEL 9 ([#29872](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29872)), discovered while working on [ocaml/odoc#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422).
- Issue [#29059](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29059) on supported OCaml compiler bounds was closed after the 4.11 archival; issues [#29569](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29569) on lwt/dune dependency expression and [#29102](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29102) on OCaml 4.10.2 Windows/Cygwin compilation were also closed this week.
repositories.discussions_summary: Debate on archiving ocaml-src dev packages without checksums; naming policy applied to doctor/ocaml-doctor submission.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Reynir Björnsson](https://github.com/reynir) opened [#29881](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29881) proposing to **archive `ocaml-src.{4.13,4.14,5.0}.dev`** packages, which have no checksums and are marked `avoid-version`. The motivation is to reduce noise in opam-mirror (related to [#22057](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/22057)); [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) agreed, noting these packages appear unused since OCaml 5.1 dropped the `.dev` style.
- [@funwithcthulhu](https://github.com/funwithcthulhu) first submitted the `ocaml-doctor` package ([#29845](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29845)) and was guided by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) and [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) to **rename it to `doctor`** per the repository's [naming policy](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/tree/master/governance/policies#7-new-package-names-should-avoid-the-ocaml-prefixsuffix) discouraging the `ocaml-` prefix; the contributor promptly reopened as [#29854](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29854).
- [Charles Averill](https://github.com/CharlesAverill) asked in [#29883](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29883) how to release two packages (`lstar-rocq` with Rocq code and `lstar` with extracted OCaml) from the same repository such that users don't need Rocq to use the extracted code, reporting that `dune-release bistro -p lstar-rocq,lstar` only released the Rocq package.
- [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) closed the draft dune 3.23.0~alpha2 PR ([#29824](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29824)) after [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) noted that concurrent large open PRs cause excessive CI recalculation in the solver on each head merge.
repositories.trends_summary: Heavy robur-coop Miou ecosystem activity; archival policy raising compiler floor; improved cross-platform depext coverage.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The **robur-coop Miou ecosystem** saw coordinated releases across multiple layers this week (miou, mkernel, mnet, mtbox, mhttp, mfat, hurl, httpcats, vif), reflecting active development of a unikernel networking stack built on the Miou scheduler ([#29855](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29855), [#29859](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29860), [#29861](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29861), [#29864](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29864), [#29865](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29865), [#29866](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29866), [#29867](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29867), [#29876](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29876)).
- The **OCaml compiler floor is rising**: the archiving policy cutoff was updated from 4.08 to 4.11 ([#29848](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29848)), and dune 3.23.0 itself raised its minimum build requirement from 4.08 to 4.14 ([#29844](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29844)). This trend is likely to continue as Debian stable moves forward.
- **Cross-platform depext coverage** continues to expand: this week added OpenBSD entries for `conf-jq` and fixed `conf-guile` for both OpenBSD and RHEL 9, plus NixOS support for `conf-cmake` ([#29871](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29871), [#29872](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29872), [#29856](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29856)).
- **Windows-specific packages** are appearing: `sha256-cng` ([#29885](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29885)) uses the native Windows CNG/BCrypt API with `available: os = "win32"`, and `ocaml_intrinsics_kernel v0.17.2` ([#29880](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29880)) fixed MSVC compilation errors, reflecting ongoing work to improve the Windows OCaml ecosystem.
repositories.issues_summary: Open issue #29158 about Windows CI building avoid-version packages remains a potential contribution target.
repositories.good_first_issues:
- Issue [#29158](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29158) (labelled **help wanted**) by [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) reports that the Windows GitHub Actions CI incorrectly attempts to build packages marked with `avoid-version`, which should be skipped. Fixing this involves adjusting the Windows CI configuration.
- Issue [#29881](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29881) (labelled **help wanted**) by [@reynir](https://github.com/reynir) proposes archiving `ocaml-src.{4.13,4.14,5.0}.dev` packages that lack checksums and appear unused. A contributor could investigate current usage and submit the companion archive PR to [ocaml/opam-repository-archive](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository-archive).
repositories.contributors_summary: 22 contributors with Calascibetta Romain leading package submissions; Hannes Mehnert handling archival and MirageOS releases; Marcello Seri reviewing throughout.
repositories.contributors:
- [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) submitted the largest volume of package releases, covering the full Miou networking stack: miou, httpcats, mkernel, mnet, mtbox, hurl, mfat, mhttp, and vif ([#29855](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29855), [#29858](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29858), [#29859](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29859), [#29860](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29860), [#29861](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29861), [#29864](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29864), [#29865](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29865), [#29866](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29866), [#29867](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29867), [#29876](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29876)).
- [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) merged the archival PR removing packages below OCaml 4.11 ([#29848](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29848)), released mirage 4.11.0 ([#29870](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29870)), ca-certs 1.0.3 ([#29869](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29869)), ca-certs-nss 3.123.1 ([#29868](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29868)), and albatross 2.7.1 ([#29851](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29851)), and provided review comments on git-net/git-kv and vif.

- [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) released dune 3.23.0 ([#29844](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29844)) and participated in the discussion about concurrent large PR CI load.
- [@chetmurthy](https://github.com/chetmurthy) released Camlp5 8.05.00 ([#29874](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29874)) and bugfix 8.04.01 ([#29884](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29884)).
- [Reynir Björnsson](https://github.com/reynir) released git-net and git-kv 0.2.2 ([#29877](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29877)) and opened the issue proposing archival of ocaml-src dev packages ([#29881](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29881)).
- [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) fixed depexts for conf-jq on OpenBSD ([#29871](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29871)) and conf-guile on OpenBSD and RHEL ([#29872](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29872)).
- [Mathieu Barbin](https://github.com/mbarbin) released cmdlang 0.0.11 (5 packages) ([#29878](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29878)).
- [@mathiasbourgoin](https://github.com/mathiasbourgoin) released miaou 0.5.2 ([#29862](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29862)) and ppx_forbid + ppx_enforce 0.1.0 ([#29853](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29853)).
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) released opam-0install 0.6.0 ([#29847](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29847)), reviewed the doctor package naming issue, and noted the CI load concern with concurrent draft PRs.
- [José Nogueira](https://github.com/thatportugueseguy) released ppx_deriving_jsonschema 0.0.7 ([#29819](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29819)) and engaged in discussion about the resulting caisar revdep issue.
- [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat) released ocaml_intrinsics_kernel v0.17.2 fixing MSVC builds ([#29880](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29880)).
- [Frédéric Recoules](https://github.com/recoules) released unisim_archisec 0.0.14 fixing x86 instruction decoding ([#29879](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29879)).
- [@funwithcthulhu](https://github.com/funwithcthulhu) submitted and then renamed the doctor diagnostic tool ([#29845](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29845), [#29854](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29854)).
- [@elfyboo](https://github.com/elfyboo) submitted sha256-cng 0.1.0 for Windows ([#29885](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29885)).
- [@filipeom](https://github.com/filipeom) added NixOS cmake depext support ([#29856](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29856)).
- [Charles Averill](https://github.com/CharlesAverill) opened [#29883](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29883) seeking guidance on releasing a dual-package (Rocq + extracted OCaml) from one repository.
