- [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) thanked the archival team and maintains open issue [#29158](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29158) on Windows CI and avoid-version packages.

repositories.brief_summary: Wasm string conversion fix, variable coalescing correctness improvements, and Node.js 26 compatibility updates merged.
repositories.new_features_summary: Wasm string conversion fallback fix, variable coalescing break-handling correction, and Node.js 26 flag removal.
repositories.new_features:
- Fixed **Wasm runtime string conversion** from JS to OCaml by adding stubs for `length` and `intoCharCodeArray` in the `string_ops` fallback, ensuring `wasm:js-string` imports resolve on engines that lack the JS String Builtins proposal ([#2230](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2230))
- Fixed **variable coalescing** incorrect handling of unlabelled `break` targeting labelled non-iteration statements (e.g. labelled blocks), and provided a more robust fix for empty `try` bodies; the CFG builder was refactored to separate `visit_stmt` from a new `visit_owning_stmt` to make context-ownership explicit ([#2233](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2233))
- Removed outdated **Node.js wrapper flags** `--experimental-wasm-imported-strings` and `--experimental-wasm-stack-switching`, which caused test failures under Node.js 26 where these Wasm features are enabled by default; only `--stack-size=10000` is retained ([#2234](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2234))
repositories.activity_summary: Two new PRs opened for dune 3.23 support and Wasm lifecycle events; four PRs merged covering Wasm, compiler, and CI fixes.
repositories.activity:
- **Draft PR opened** by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) to switch the build system to **dune 3.23**, which uses `js_of_ocaml --build-config` to manage configuration details; currently blocked on upstream dune issues [ocaml/dune#14441](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14441) and [ocaml/dune#14438](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14438), and currently in conflict ([#2231](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2231))
- **New PR opened** by [Jason Gross](https://github.com/JasonGross) proposing that the Wasm launcher **dispatch `wasmoocaml:loaded` and `wasmoocaml:error` CustomEvents** on `globalThis` when async Wasm instantiation completes or fails, allowing external code to wait for the OCaml runtime to be ready; ported from [ocaml-wasm/wasm_of_ocaml#143](https://github.com/ocaml-wasm/wasm_of_ocaml/issues/143), currently has merge conflicts ([#2232](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2232))
- **CI fix merged** by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) addressing a dune 3.23 compatibility issue where a lower bound on the menhir dependency was newly enforced ([#2236](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2236))
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) opened [#2235](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2235) to fix Node.js 26 support by removing the same outdated experimental Wasm flags; this was closed in favour of [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon)'s equivalent [#2234](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2234) which was merged the same day
- Long-running draft PRs for **Wasm native effects** (Stack Switching proposal, blocked on dune release) and a full **WASI runtime** remain open and pending unblocking dependencies ([#2189](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2189), [#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831))
- [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon)'s draft PR to **extract loops from the toplevel function** into helper functions for Wasm tier-up optimisation remains open ([#2212](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2212))
repositories.discussions_summary: Issue discussions touched on neutral target-env support for runtimes like QuickJS, and dune 3.23.1 backward compatibility with OCaml 4.13.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu)'s feature request for a **`--target-env=neutral`** mode targeting non-browser/non-Node.js runtimes like QuickJS received a pointer from [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) to [#2216](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2216) for telling esbuild to ignore certain `require` calls ([#2226](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2226))
- In [#2231](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2231), [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) noted that **dune 3.23.1** should restore compatibility with OCaml 4.13, referencing [ocaml/dune#14443](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14443), and also flagged a potential use of `%{dep:wc.ml}` as a build system improvement
- [Jason Gross](https://github.com/JasonGross) noted in [#2232](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2232) that CI failures on the new Wasm lifecycle events PR appear spurious
repositories.trends_summary: Continued focus on Wasm correctness, Node.js version compatibility, and modernising the build system toward dune 3.23.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Wasm correctness** continues to be a focus area, with fixes for string conversion fallbacks ([#2230](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2230)), ongoing native effects work ([#2189](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2189)), WASI runtime development ([#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831)), and a new proposal for async instantiation lifecycle events ([#2232](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2232))
- **Node.js version tracking**: two parallel PRs ([#2234](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2234), [#2235](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2235)) appeared simultaneously to remove experimental flags that Node.js 26 now enables by default, reflecting active monitoring of Node.js releases
- **Dune upgrade friction**: the blocked [#2231](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2231) and the related CI fix [#2236](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2236) show active work to adopt dune 3.23 while waiting on upstream fixes in [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)
repositories.contributors_summary: Jérôme Vouillon drove merges and new PRs; hhugo provided reviews and opened a competing fix; Jason Gross contributed a new Wasm feature PR.
repositories.contributors:
- [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) merged four PRs: Wasm string conversion fix ([#2230](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2230)), Node wrapper flag removal ([#2234](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2234)), CI fix for dune 3.23 ([#2236](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2236)), and variable coalescing break-instruction fix ([#2233](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2233)); also opened draft PRs for dune 3.23 migration ([#2231](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2231)) and previously opened the OCaml 5.6 support draft ([#2227](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2227))
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) reviewed [#2231](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2231) (suggesting `%{dep:wc.ml}` and noting dune 3.23.1 OCaml 4.13 compatibility), provided review feedback on [#2233](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2233) (proposing the `visit_stmt`/`visit_owning_stmt` split that was subsequently adopted), and opened [#2235](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2235) to fix Node.js 26 compatibility (closed in favour of [#2234](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2234))
- [Jason Gross](https://github.com/JasonGross) opened [#2232](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2232) proposing Wasm lifecycle event dispatching (`wasmoocaml:loaded`/`wasmoocaml:error`), ported from the upstream [ocaml-wasm/wasm_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml-wasm/wasm_of_ocaml) repository
- [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) provided detailed feedback on feature requests for a neutral compilation target ([#2226](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2226)) and runtime feature detection ([#2215](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2215)), both receiving responses during the week

repositories.brief_summary: Merlin integrated as a repo subtree, ikinds enabled by default, and active 5.4 merge preparation dominate the week.
repositories.new_features_summary: ikinds on by default, implicit kind redeclaration, doc comments on kind declarations, shorter AVX/VEX encodings, and better primitive error messages.
repositories.new_features:
- **ikinds enabled by default**: the `-ikinds` flag is replaced by `-no-ikinds`; for all tests with both jkind and ikind variants, `-no-ikinds` is added to the jkind test and `-ikinds` removed from the ikind test — credit to [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) for the implementation and [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) for the switch ([#5999](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5999)); also, a fix for **ikind substitution with abstract kind constraints** (`KAtom` references going stale after `with kind_ k =` constraints) was merged as a prerequisite ([#5998](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5998))
- **Allow implicit kind redeclaration**: modules can now contain nested `[@@@implicit_kind]` annotations that differ from the outer declaration — previously rejected conservatively — by [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) ([#5980](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5980))
- **Doc comments on kind declarations** now preserved: a bug that silently dropped `(** ... *)` documentation on kind declarations is fixed by [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin) ([#5987](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5987))
- **Better error messages for `Invalid_native_repr_for_primitive`**: errors now include a `Hint:` line pointing users to the `[@unpacked]` attribute when product layouts appear in C stub arguments, by [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4) ([#5985](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5985))
- **Fix modes bug in untypeast/pprintast roundtrip**: modalities on `val` and `external` signature/structure items were not restored by `untypeast`, causing files to fail typechecking after the roundtrip; fixed by [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin) ([#5986](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5986))
- **Fix hidden dependency cache checking**: `Persistent_env.check` now applies the visibility predicate to cached entries before treating a module as available, making `-H` hidden-dependency warning behavior consistent regardless of cache state and lookup order, by [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) ([#6020](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6020))
- **Shorter AVX/VEX encodings in the x86 binary emitter**: support for 2-byte VEX prefix (instead of 3-byte) when opcode map, W-bit, and register extension bits permit it ([#6012](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6012)); shorter `vmovq` and `movapd`/`movupd` encodings ([#6022](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6022)); prefer 2-byte VEX form for YMM reg-reg moves ([#6033](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6033)) — all by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon)
- **Patricia trees use GADTs to encode invariants**: the `Empty` constructor can now only appear at toplevel, enabling allocation-free `Set` operations; `Set.union` code size halved according to generated Flambda output, by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs) ([#5899](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5899))
- **Merlin added as a git subtree** at `external/merlin/`, bringing the full Merlin history into the oxcaml monorepo with CI integration and an updated import script, by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) ([#5568](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5568))
- **Syntactic sugar for fexpr**: `switch` branches can now inline `goto` handler code directly, reducing verbosity in Flambda2 expect tests, by [Keryan Didier](https://github.com/Keryan-dev) ([#5474](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5474))
- **fexpr: Display stub information** for code symbols in Flambda2 intermediate representation output, by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) ([#6004](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6004))
repositories.activity_summary: 5.4 merge work, x86 binary emitter correctness, Merlin build rules, layout-poly PRs, and metaprogramming unloading all advance this week.
repositories.activity:
- Several **newly opened PRs target 5.4 merge preparation**: [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) opened fixes for DWARF SLEB128 encoding ([#6021](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6021)), preserving the frame pointer register at noalloc C calls ([#6014](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6014)), and effects typechecking ([#6001](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6001) by [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js)); the combined integration PR ([#5958](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5958)) remains open as a staging ground
- [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) opened a **metaprogramming compilation unit unloading** PR ([#5960](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5960)) introducing a mark/sweep GC approach for unloadable CUs with code blocks as heap-shaped objects, back-pointers in `.text`, and frame-descriptor flag bits
- [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet) opened the **SSA intermediate representation** PR ([#5810](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5810)) introducing a new backend pass between Cmm and CFG with a `-use-ssa` flag, a GADT-typed graph API, and a `Cfg_compare` validator for register equivalence
- [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) opened a series of **x86 binary emitter correctness PRs**: NOP encoding fixes and text-section alignment ([#6028](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6028), [#6029](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6029)), monotone backwards jumps ([#6025](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6025)), shorter 16-bit immediate arithmetic ([#6027](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6027)), and removal of a jump distance heuristic ([#6024](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6024))
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) followed up the Merlin subtree merge with **build rules for Merlin** ([#6042](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6042)), an updated import script ([#6034](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6034)), and migration of Merlin PRs into the monorepo: signature help fix ([#6045](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6045)) and labelled-argument tests ([#6044](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6044)) from Charlène Gros
- **Layout-poly work** advanced via [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr)'s PRs to provide layout-poly static data via callbacks ([#6049](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6049)) and split `Compilenv` for lpoly use ([#6030](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6030)); [Andrej Ivašković](https://github.com/Dreian) opened a PR to print `poly_` in value descriptions ([#6038](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6038)); and [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) opened persistent-module staticity via CMI modalities ([#6041](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6041))
- [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs) opened **reaper bug fixes** for set-of-closures rewriting ([#6005](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6005)) and a draft improvement to call-removal accounting during inlining ([#5991](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5991))
- [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) opened draft Flambda2 improvements: **affine switch recognition** ([#6048](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6048)) and **CSE of coeffectful expressions** ([#6036](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6036))
- [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) opened a fix for a **compiler panic with `[@@unboxed]` and existential types** ([#6046](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6046)); [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) added a CI check for conflict markers ([#6051](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6051))
- [Jakub Staroń](https://github.com/staronj) opened **`-open-cmi`**, a new compiler flag to open a module signature directly from a `.cmi` file without consulting `-I`/`-H` paths ([#6013](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6013))
- [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat) opened a PR to **record fiber IDs for magic-trace** via PTWRITE instruction when Intel PT is active ([#6032](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6032))
- **Reaper points-to analysis refactoring** was merged: [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) landed more principled access functions for usages and sources ([#5938](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5938))
- **`Cfg_quick_hash` module** extracted from `Cfg_merge_blocks` with hashes that ignore constructor payloads, for passes interested in equality up to renaming, by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) ([#5788](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5788))
- **Record representation cleanup**: `Record_boxed` no longer stores an array of sorts (dead info since upstream changes), by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) ([#6002](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6002)); [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) also opened a follow-up refactoring of record representation recomputation ahead of allowing `any`-kinded fields ([#6035](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6035))
- Several long-running open PRs remain in review: **zero-alloc for HOF arguments** ([#5493](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5493)) by [Andrej Ivašković](https://github.com/Dreian), **allow fields of kind `any`** ([#5461](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5461)) and **`option` kind change** ([#5462](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5462)) by [Luke Maurer](https://github.com/lukemaurer), **optimized Patricia trees** ([#5627](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5627)) by [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs), and **`[@@or_null]` GADTs** ([#5828](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5828), [#5830](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5830)) by [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs)
repositories.discussions_summary: Debate around Hashtable vs Map for the solver, merlin build workflow trade-offs, and SLEB128 DWARF encoding analysis generated notable technical discussion.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) and [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) discussed the correctness and performance implications of replacing `Map` with `Hashtable` in the mode solver ([#5962](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5962)), noting a 3% regression; [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) also opened a separate fix using a **total structural order** for solver morph map keys to avoid the unsound partial ordering ([#6047](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6047))
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) and [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) had an extended discussion in [#5568](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5568) around the **vendored vs. explicit Merlin build workflow**, how `dune-workspace` files interact with the boot/runtime_stdlib/main workspaces, and why the boot context uses the `default` name to keep editor tooling working
- [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist)'s DWARF SLEB128 fix ([#6021](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6021)) includes a detailed analysis of why frame-pointer builds were unaffected (offset 56 fits in single SLEB128 byte, offset 72 without frame pointers does not), prompting discussion with [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js)
- [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) and [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) discussed the `borrow_` operator documentation PR ([#5928](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5928)), debating whether borrowing aliased values should be advertised given it's an implementation artifact rather than a designed feature
repositories.trends_summary: 5.4 upstream merge preparation, x86 binary emitter correctness, Merlin monorepo integration, and metaprogramming runtime infrastructure all see coordinated pushes this week.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **5.4 merge preparation** is intensifying: multiple PRs ([#6021](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6021), [#6014](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6014), [#5989](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5989), [#5988](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5988), [#5972](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5972), [#6015](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6015), [#5958](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5958)) target the 5.4 branch, with the draft integration PR [#5958](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5958) serving as a staging area
- **Binary emitter correctness** is a clear focus: [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) landed three VEX encoding PRs and opened five more in a single week, suggesting systematic review of the x86 emitter for correctness issues in NOP padding, jump encoding, and prefix selection ([#6012](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6012), [#6022](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6022), [#6033](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6033), [#6028](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6028), [#6029](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6029), [#6027](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6027), [#6025](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6025), [#6024](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6024))
- **Merlin monorepo integration** is becoming a project in itself: after the subtree merge ([#5568](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5568)), there are immediate follow-ups for build rules ([#6042](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6042)), import script improvements ([#6034](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6034)), and migration of existing Merlin PRs ([#6044](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6044), [#6045](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6045))
- **Metaprogramming infrastructure** continues to grow: binary emitter tests (#5942), fexpr syntactic sugar ([#5474](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5474)), stub display ([#6004](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6004)), and the large unloading PR ([#5960](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5960)) all target the `100% vibe` / `metaprogramming` label set
- **Layout polymorphism** is advancing on multiple fronts simultaneously: `val poly_` printing ([#6038](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6038)), layout variables in product kinds ([#6026](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6026)), `Compilenv` split ([#6030](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6030)), static data via callbacks ([#6049](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6049)), and the foundational `any`-kind PRs ([#5461](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5461), [#5462](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5462))
repositories.contributors_summary: 24 contributors were active across code, reviews, and discussions, with Simon Spies, David Allsopp, and Liam Stevenson particularly prolific this week.

repositories.brief_summary: The oxcaml/merlin repo was deprecated as OxCaml Merlin moves to oxcaml/oxcaml, with misc.ml synced to compiler and Nix flake updated.
repositories.new_features_summary: Repository deprecated with redirect README; misc.ml restructured to match compiler layout; Nix flake updated to OCaml 5.4.
repositories.new_features:
- The **oxcaml/merlin repository was deprecated** by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923): nearly all contents were removed and the README replaced with a pointer to [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) as the canonical home for OxCaml Merlin (since [oxcaml/oxcaml#5568](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5568)) ([#240](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/240))
- `src/utils/misc.ml(i)` and `src/utils/format_doc.ml(i)` were **moved to `src/ocaml/utils/`** to align the file layout with the upstream compiler, and the erroneously introduced `misc_stdlib.ml(i)` was **deleted** as part of syncing with [ocaml/merlin#2059](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2059) ([#238](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/238))
- The **Nix flake was updated to OCaml 5.4** (from 5.2) with NixPkgs pinned to February 14 to avoid a `menhirGLR` package conflict ([#236](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/236))
repositories.activity_summary: Deprecation of this standalone repo and migration of in-flight work to oxcaml/oxcaml; two open PRs closed and redirected upstream.
repositories.activity:
- Draft PR [#239](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/239) by [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) titled **"[5.4] Merge minus-37"** remains open with merge conflicts (424k additions, 74k deletions across 888 files), representing a large pending synchronization that predates the deprecation
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) **merged the `misc.ml` synchronization** ([#238](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/238)) bringing Merlin's utility modules in line with the OxCaml compiler before closing the repo, with review by [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js)
- Two PRs by [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) were **closed without merging** and redirected to the new canonical location: the `let..in` signature-help fix ([#223](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/223)) moved to [oxcaml/oxcaml#6045](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6045), and the signature-help tests for labelled arguments ([#205](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/205)) moved to [oxcaml/oxcaml#6044](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6044)
repositories.discussions_summary: Closure comments on two PRs explained the migration strategy to oxcaml/oxcaml as the new canonical home.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) left closing comments on [#223](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/223) and [#205](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/205) directing contributors to re-open their work in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml), providing links to the corresponding replacement PRs ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6045](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6045) and [oxcaml/oxcaml#6044](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6044))
- [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) commented on [#238](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/238) that a review workflow showing conflicts before and after resolution would be preferred, noting they had to recreate the process locally to review
repositories.trends_summary: Development of OxCaml Merlin has consolidated into oxcaml/oxcaml, ending the standalone fork model.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The **consolidation of OxCaml Merlin into the monorepo** at [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) (since [oxcaml/oxcaml#5568](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5568)) marks a shift away from maintaining a separate fork; future Merlin contributions for OxCaml should target that repository
- The `misc.ml` synchronization effort ([#238](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/238)) and its upstream at [ocaml/merlin#2059](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2059) reflect an **ongoing effort to reduce divergence** between the OxCaml Merlin codebase and the upstream OCaml compiler utilities
repositories.contributors_summary: Liam Stevenson drove the deprecation and misc.ml sync; Gavin Gray updated the Nix flake; Charlène_Gros had two PRs redirected upstream.
repositories.contributors:
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) authored and merged the repository deprecation PR ([#240](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/240)) and the `misc.ml` synchronization ([#238](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/238)), and closed the in-flight PRs from other contributors with guidance toward [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml)
- [Gavin Gray](https://github.com/gavinleroy) authored and had merged the Nix flake update to OCaml 5.4 ([#236](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/236))
- [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) reviewed [#238](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/238) and has an open draft PR ([#239](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/239)) for a large 5.4 merge
- [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) had two PRs closed this week ([#223](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/223), [#205](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/205)) with their work redirected to [oxcaml/oxcaml#6045](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6045) and [oxcaml/oxcaml#6044](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6044)

repositories.brief_summary: Package additions and cleanup: extlib 1.8.0+ox, dune 3.22.2+ox, odoc 3.2.0+ox added; deprecated odoc 3.1.0+ox patches removed.
repositories.new_features_summary: New packages: extlib 1.8.0+ox, dune 3.22.2+ox, odoc 3.2.0+ox, opam-core/format 2.5.1+ox, and oxcaml.action-required virtual package.
repositories.new_features:
- New **`extlib.1.8.0+ox`** package added with an OxCaml-specific compatibility patch, enabling extlib to build under OxCaml ([#44](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/44))
- New **`oxcaml.action-required`** virtual package added to guide users who try to install an OxCaml-dependent package without the overlay repository enabled, providing actionable error messages and setup instructions ([#40](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/40))
- New **`dune.3.22.2+ox`** package added alongside **`odoc.3.2.0+ox`**, `odoc-driver.3.2.0+ox`, `odoc-md.3.2.0+ox`, `odoc-parser.3.2.0+ox`, and `sherlodoc.3.2.0+ox` — this upgrade resolves a CI breakage since dune 3.22 is required for OxCaml detection by the latest odoc ([#39](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/39))
- New **`opam-core.2.5.1+ox`** and **`opam-format.2.5.1+ox`** packages added to keep the overlay up to date with the upstream opam 2.5.1 release ([#41](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/41))
- **`opam-core.2.5.0+ox`** patch updated to support `oxcaml-compiler 5.2.0minus31` and the corresponding upper-bound conflict removed ([#41](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/41))
- Deprecated **`odoc.3.1.0+ox`**, `odoc-driver.3.1.0+ox`, `odoc-md.3.1.0+ox`, `odoc-parser.3.1.0+ox`, and `sherlodoc.3.1.0+ox`** packages removed from the overlay; replaced with a `conflict: odoc < 3.2.0` constraint, as odoc no longer requires OxCaml-specific patches ([#43](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/43))
repositories.activity_summary: Two PRs remain open: ocamlbuild Dune compatibility patch under review and a bootstrap compiler update flagged as possibly obsolete.
repositories.activity:
- **Open PR awaiting review**: [Sudha Parimala](https://github.com/Sudha247) opened [#21](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/21) adding a Dune package management compatibility patch for `ocamlbuild`; [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) has left detailed review comments questioning whether the patch is still needed and suggesting cleaner approaches using `extra-source` and opam variable filters
- **Open PR flagged as potentially obsolete**: [Eduardo Souza](https://github.com/udesou) opened [#36](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/36) to add OCaml 5.4.1 as a bootstrap compiler in the variants opam file; [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) noted this may no longer be required following changes allowing multiple compiler versions, and the PR currently has merge conflicts
- **Closed without merging**: [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w)'s [#42](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/42) adding `opam-format.2.5.1+ox` was superseded by [#44](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/44) which took a broader approach; [Sudha Parimala](https://github.com/Sudha247)'s [#22](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/22) adding a Dune compatibility patch for `odoc-parser` was closed as unnecessary since the upstream PR merged and odoc no longer needs OxCaml-specific patches
- **Issue closed**: [#35](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/35) from [Nirnay Roy](https://github.com/nirnayroy) asking how to publish an opam package depending on oxcaml was closed by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js), who confirmed that the necessary infrastructure is now in place in both this overlay and the upstream opam-repository
repositories.discussions_summary: PR #21 discussion explored whether the ocamlbuild Dune patch is still needed and proposed cleaner alternatives using extra-source and opam variable filters.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) raised detailed technical questions on [#21](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/21) about the `ocamlbuild` Dune compatibility patch, suggesting the patch could be simplified by using `extra-source` to reference an upstream commit directly and proposing use of a `with-dune-package-management` opam variable to conditionally apply the patch — pointing to [ocaml/ocamlbuild#362](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlbuild/issues/362) and [ocaml/ocamlbuild#327](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlbuild/issues/327) for upstream context
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) confirmed on issue [#35](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/35) that the infrastructure for third-party packages depending on OxCaml is now fully in place, providing closure for contributors wishing to publish OxCaml-dependent packages
repositories.trends_summary: Steady reduction of OxCaml-specific patch overhead as upstream projects incorporate OxCaml compatibility, with the overlay shifting toward version constraints.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Upstream absorption of OxCaml patches**: The removal of odoc 3.1.0+ox packages and the closure of the odoc-parser compatibility PR reflect a pattern where OxCaml-specific patches are being upstreamed and released, allowing the overlay to replace custom-patched packages with plain version constraints (e.g., `conflict: odoc < 3.2.0`) — reducing maintenance burden ([#43](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/43), [#22](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/22))
- **Growing opam ecosystem coverage**: New OxCaml-patched variants of `extlib`, `dune 3.22.2`, and `opam-core/format 2.5.1` indicate continued broadening of the package ecosystem supported under OxCaml ([#44](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/44), [#39](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/39), [#41](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/41))
- **Improved onboarding infrastructure**: The `oxcaml.action-required` virtual package mirrors [ocaml/opam-repository#29809](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29809) and suggests a more systematic approach to guiding new users toward the correct setup path when they encounter OxCaml-dependent packages without the overlay ([#40](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/40))
repositories.contributors_summary: David Allsopp drove the week's reviews and merges; @art-w and Jérôme Vouillon contributed new and updated packages.

repositories.brief_summary: Feature request for `Eio.Promise.map` opened, with design discussion on promise/fiber semantics.
repositories.activity_summary: A feature request for `Eio.Promise.map` prompted discussion on promise design trade-offs.
repositories.activity: - [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) opened [#838](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/838) proposing an **`Eio.Promise.map`** function to allow mapping promises without requiring an attached switch, noting that the existing workaround using `Fiber.fork ~sw` breaks the switchless promise API design.
repositories.discussions_summary: Design discussion on `Eio.Promise.map` explored where mapped fiber code would run and concurrency concerns.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) responded to [#838](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/838) explaining the core difficulty: **promise resolution currently runs no user code** (safe even from signal handlers), so the domain that resolves a promise cannot run `f`; if the mapping domain handles it, a switch is still needed; and lazy evaluation introduces **locking concerns** when multiple domains await the mapped promise simultaneously. He noted `Condition.register_immediate` exists but is intended for IO library integration rather than general use.
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) acknowledged the constraints and noted his specific use case is a non-yielding immediate transformation (analogous to `Option.get`), concluding a switch is acceptable for his scenario ([#838](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/838)).
repositories.trends_summary: Interest in enriching the promise API while preserving its switchless, concurrency-safe design constraints.
repositories.emerging_trends: - The discussion around [#838](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/838) reflects ongoing interest in making **Eio's promise primitives** more ergonomic for functional-style composition, while the response highlights the deliberate design choice to keep promise resolution free of user-code execution for safety and simplicity.
repositories.contributors_summary: Patrick Ferris opened and discussed issue #838; Thomas Leonard provided design context in comments.
repositories.contributors:
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) opened [#838](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/838) proposing `Eio.Promise.map`, including a working workaround implementation and reasoning about the desired switchless API behaviour.
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) provided a detailed explanation of the design constraints preventing a straightforward implementation in the comments on [#838](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/838).

group_summaries.brief_summary: OCaml 4.11 archival cutoff enforced, dune 3.23.0 released and documented, and robur-coop Miou ecosystem packages published in bulk.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: dune 3.23.0 adds sandboxed rules by default; OCaml <4.11 packages archived; platform release changelogs and AbsInt added to ocaml.org.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **OCaml compiler archival cutoff raised to 4.11**: [ocaml/opam-repository#29848](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29848) removed 1,132 package definition files (53,000+ lines) for compilers below 4.11, with a companion archival PR at [ocaml/opam-repository-archive#35](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository-archive/issues/35).
- **dune 3.23.0** released via [ocaml/opam-repository#29844](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29844): user rules are now sandboxed by default, `dune clean` accepts target arguments, `dune promote` promotes all paths matching a prefix, and `compile_commands.json` is generated for C/C++ foreign stubs when building `@check`. Minimum OCaml version to build dune raised from 4.08 to 4.14. Release announcement published on ocaml.org via [ocaml/ocaml.org#3637](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3637).
- **miou 0.6.0** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29855](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29855)) adds a `Miou.Trace` module with runtime event integration and `Miou.take` for consuming tasks from `Miou.orphans`.
- **vif 0.0.1~beta3** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29876](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29876)) adds UNIX domain socket listener support, graceful server shutdown, and an OPTIONS route method.
- **Camlp5 8.05.00** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29874](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29874)) adds OCaml 5.5.0 syntax support; bugfix **8.04.01** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29884](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29884)) corrects labeled tuple handling in constructor expressions.
- **ppx_deriving_jsonschema 0.0.7** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29819](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29819)) adds `[@@jsonschema.compact_variants]`, `[@@jsonschema.format]`, `[@@jsonschema.description]`, `[@jsonschema.default]`, and `[@jsonschema.attrs]` attributes, and makes `option` types nullable.
- **art 0.4.0** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29873](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29873)) adds the `Art.map` function to the Adaptive Radix Tree library.
- **`CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`** formally added to opam-repository ([ocaml/opam-repository#29850](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29850)), adopting the [OCaml Code of Conduct](https://github.com/ocaml/code-of-conduct).
- **AbsInt** added to the ocaml.org industrial users page via [ocaml/ocaml.org#3647](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3647), documenting their use of OCaml in the Astrée static analyser and CompCert compiler.
- Draft release changelog pages added to ocaml.org for **dune-release 2.2.1**, **mdx 2.5.2**, **merlin v5.7.0-504 / v5.7.1-504 / v5.7-505~preview**, **ocaml-lsp 1.26.0-5.5~preview**, **ocamlformat 0.29.0**, **OCaml 5.5.0~alpha2 / ~beta1**, **odoc 3.2.0**, **opam-publish 3.0.0**, and **utop 2.17.0** via [ocaml/ocaml.org#3641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3641).
- **`conf-jq`** extended with OpenBSD package name ([ocaml/opam-repository#29871](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29871)), **`conf-cmake`** extended with NixOS depext support ([ocaml/opam-repository#29856](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29856)), and **`conf-guile`** corrected for OpenBSD and RHEL 9 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29872](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29872)).
group_summaries.group_overview:
- **ocaml/opam-repository** saw its largest structural change of recent weeks with the archival of all packages requiring OCaml < 4.11 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29848](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29848)), removing over 53,000 lines and 1,132 files.
- **dune 3.23.0** was released on opam-repository ([ocaml/opam-repository#29844](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29844)) and its release announcement published on ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3637](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3637)), representing coordinated activity across both repositories.
- [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) submitted nine coordinated releases for the robur-coop Miou networking stack, spanning scheduler, kernel, network, HTTP, FAT, and web server layers.
- **ocaml/ocaml.org** focused on retroactively filling platform release documentation gaps: draft changelog pages were added for 12+ releases and alpha series data tracked with `ignore: true` for newsletter use ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3640](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3640), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3641)).
- Three new standalone packages were accepted: **doctor 0.1.0** (OCaml environment diagnostics, [ocaml/opam-repository#29854](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29854)), **ppx_forbid + ppx_enforce 0.1.0** (compile-time access control PPX, [ocaml/opam-repository#29853](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29853)), and **sha256-cng 0.1.0** (Windows-native SHA-256, [ocaml/opam-repository#29885](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29885)).
- Cross-platform depext coverage expanded with OpenBSD and RHEL 9 fixes for conf-jq ([ocaml/opam-repository#29871](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29871)), conf-guile ([ocaml/opam-repository#29872](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29872)), and NixOS support for conf-cmake ([ocaml/opam-repository#29856](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29856)).
group_summaries.activity_summary: 22 contributors active on opam-repository; two PRs remain open on ocaml.org awaiting maintainer confirmation for platform release changelogs.
group_summaries.activity:
- [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) merged the archival PR ([ocaml/opam-repository#29848](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29848)) and released mirage 4.11.0 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29870](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29870)), ca-certs 1.0.3 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29869](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29869)), ca-certs-nss 3.123.1 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29868](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29868)), and albatross 2.7.1 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29851](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29851)).
- [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) released the full Miou networking stack: miou 0.6.0 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29855](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29855)), httpcats 0.3.0 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29859](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29859)) with revdeps prep ([ocaml/opam-repository#29858](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29858)), mkernel 0.0.2 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29860](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29860)), mnet 0.0.2 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29861](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29861)), mtbox 0.0.1~beta1 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29864](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29864)), hurl 0.0.1~beta2 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29865](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29865)), mfat 0.0.1~beta1 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29866](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29866)), mhttp 0.0.2 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29867](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29867)), and vif 0.0.1~beta3 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29876](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29876)).
- [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) released dune 3.23.0 (18 packages) to opam-repository ([ocaml/opam-repository#29844](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29844)), closing the earlier draft alpha PR ([ocaml/opam-repository#29824](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29824)) to avoid CI overload, and authored the dune 3.23.0 release announcement on ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3637](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3637)).
- [@sabine](https://github.com/sabine) opened the batch draft platform release changelog PR ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3641)), added dune alpha release data ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3640](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3640)), added AbsInt to industrial users ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3647](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3647)), and clarified contribution guidelines for industrial user listings ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3645](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3645)).
- [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri) reviewed multiple PRs, adjusted Camlp5 OCaml version bounds ([ocaml/opam-repository#29875](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29875)), applied the caisar upper-bound fix for ppx_deriving_jsonschema ([ocaml/opam-repository#29849](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29849)), and added the formal CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md ([ocaml/opam-repository#29850](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29850)).
- [@chetmurthy](https://github.com/chetmurthy) released Camlp5 8.05.00 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29874](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29874)) and bugfix 8.04.01 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29884](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29884)).
- [Reynir Björnsson](https://github.com/reynir) released git-net and git-kv 0.2.2 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29877](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29877)) and opened [ocaml/opam-repository#29881](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29881) proposing archival of ocaml-src dev packages.
- [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) fixed depexts for conf-jq on OpenBSD ([ocaml/opam-repository#29871](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29871)) and conf-guile on OpenBSD and RHEL 9 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29872](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29872)), triggered by work on [ocaml/odoc#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422).

- [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat) released ocaml_intrinsics_kernel v0.17.2 fixing MSVC compilation errors ([ocaml/opam-repository#29880](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29880)); [Frédéric Recoules](https://github.com/recoules) released unisim_archisec 0.0.14 fixing x86 instruction decoding segfaults ([ocaml/opam-repository#29879](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29879)).
