- [Sacha Ayoun](https://github.com/giltho) added the Caml in the Capital recurring meetup and corrected the S-REPLS location on ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3633](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3633)).
- [Anton Melnikov](https://github.com/botantony) fixed a missing space in the values-and-functions tutorial ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3639](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3639)).
- [Tobias Mock](https://github.com/tjammer) opened a documentation update to add the `--with-dev-setup` flag to the managing dependencies tutorial ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3646](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3646)), currently open.
- Issues [ocaml/opam-repository#29059](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29059), [ocaml/opam-repository#29569](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29569), and [ocaml/opam-repository#29102](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29102) were closed following the 4.11 archival and related cleanup.
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **dune 3.23.0** was released to [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29844](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29844)) and simultaneously documented on [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3637](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3637)), with [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) authoring both the opam release and the website announcement.
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) contributed to both repositories this week: releasing opam-0install 0.6.0 on opam-repository ([ocaml/opam-repository#29847](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29847)) and reviewing the dune 3.23.0 release announcement on ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3637](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3637)).
- The dune 3.23.0 release announcement on ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3637](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3637)) specifically documents the new `--with-dev-setup` opam install flag, which is also the subject of an open tutorial improvement PR ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3646](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3646)).
- The batch of draft platform release changelogs on ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3641)) covers releases for tools whose packages appear in opam-repository, including ocamlformat 0.29.0, odoc 3.2.0, merlin, ocaml-lsp, and opam-publish 3.0.0.
- [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV)'s depext fixes for conf-guile ([ocaml/opam-repository#29872](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29872)) were triggered by work on [ocaml/odoc#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422), illustrating how odoc development surfaces opam depext gaps across platforms.
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- [Jules Aguillon](https://github.com/Julow) flagged in [ocaml/ocaml.org#3641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3641) that a code block in the draft mdx 2.5.2 changelog was malformed and suggested removing an inaccurate AI-generated section rather than publishing summary text that diverges from the actual changelog.
- [@sabine](https://github.com/sabine) raised a process concern in [ocaml/ocaml.org#3641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3641): release announcements should ideally be written by maintainers on discuss.ocaml.org first, and the current stop-gap approach of drafting pages from changelog data cannot be verified without maintainer input.
- [Reynir Björnsson](https://github.com/reynir) opened [ocaml/opam-repository#29881](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29881) proposing to archive `ocaml-src.{4.13,4.14,5.0}.dev` packages that have no checksums and are marked `avoid-version`, to reduce noise in opam-mirror; [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) agreed they appear unused since OCaml 5.1 dropped the `.dev` style.
- [@funwithcthulhu](https://github.com/funwithcthulhu) submitted the `ocaml-doctor` package ([ocaml/opam-repository#29845](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29845)) and was guided by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) and [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) to rename it to `doctor` per the repository's naming policy discouraging the `ocaml-` prefix; the contributor promptly reopened as [ocaml/opam-repository#29854](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29854).
- [Charles Averill](https://github.com/CharlesAverill) asked in [ocaml/opam-repository#29883](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29883) how to release two related packages (`lstar-rocq` with Rocq code and `lstar` with extracted OCaml) from the same repository so users do not need Rocq installed, reporting that `dune-release bistro -p lstar-rocq,lstar` only released the Rocq package; the issue remains open.
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **The OCaml compiler floor is rising**: the archiving policy cutoff was updated from 4.08 to 4.11 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29848](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29848)), and dune 3.23.0 itself raised its minimum build requirement from 4.08 to 4.14 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29844](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29844)). A further cleanup of ocaml-src dev packages without checksums is under discussion ([ocaml/opam-repository#29881](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29881)).
- **The robur-coop Miou ecosystem** produced coordinated releases across nine packages (miou, mkernel, mnet, mtbox, mhttp, mfat, hurl, httpcats, vif) in a single week ([ocaml/opam-repository#29855](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29855), [ocaml/opam-repository#29860](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29860), [ocaml/opam-repository#29861](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29861), [ocaml/opam-repository#29864](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29864), [ocaml/opam-repository#29865](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29865), [ocaml/opam-repository#29866](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29866), [ocaml/opam-repository#29867](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29867), [ocaml/opam-repository#29876](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29876)), reflecting active development of a unikernel networking stack built on the Miou scheduler.
- **Platform release documentation is catching up**: multiple platform tool releases from February–May 2026 had no corresponding ocaml.org announcement pages; [ocaml/ocaml.org#3641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3641) and [ocaml/ocaml.org#3642](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3642) represent an effort to retroactively document these, while dune alpha changelogs are now tracked with `ignore: true` ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3640](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3640)) to avoid premature publication.
- **Cross-platform depext coverage is expanding**: this week added OpenBSD entries for conf-jq ([ocaml/opam-repository#29871](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29871)), fixed conf-guile for OpenBSD and RHEL 9 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29872](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29872)), and added NixOS support for conf-cmake ([ocaml/opam-repository#29856](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29856)).
- **Windows-specific OCaml packages are growing**: sha256-cng 0.1.0 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29885](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29885)) uses the native Windows CNG/BCrypt API, and ocaml_intrinsics_kernel v0.17.2 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29880](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29880)) fixed MSVC compilation errors, continuing a pattern of increased Windows ecosystem investment.

group_summaries.brief_summary: Dune 3.23.0 released with 3.23.1 regression cycle underway; opam fixes 7-year-old update bug; Merlin rename propagated to ocaml-lsp.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Dune 3.23.0 shipped relocatable compiler support, sandbox throttling, and a new RPC flush-file-watcher command; opam update handles file/dir renames.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) 3.23.0 released** ([ocaml/dune#14212](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14212)): shipped 2026-05-05 by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder), including user-rule sandboxing by default, `compile_commands.json` for C/C++ stubs, shared jsoo runtimes, hardlink sandboxing on Windows, and `dune clean` target arguments.
- **Relocatable compiler in `dune pkg`** ([ocaml/dune#14357](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14357)): dune now detects and uses the relocatable OCaml compiler from David Allsopp's opam repository, enabling the compiler to be treated as a cacheable package and closing [ocaml/dune#13229](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13229) and [ocaml/dune#14012](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14012).
- **`dune rpc flush-file-watcher` command** ([ocaml/dune#14467](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14467)): new RPC procedure allowing clients to synchronise with the file watcher before issuing build requests, added to dune-rpc's public API.
- **RPC connection status in build status line** ([ocaml/dune#14424](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14424)): the build status line now shows a `[rpc N]` section; `Console.Status_line` gained `add_section`/`remove_section` APIs for composable persistent fragments.
- **Live sandbox throttle** ([ocaml/dune#14464](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14464)): a global limit of 250 slots prevents unbounded concurrent sandbox directory creation, reducing disk pressure during large parallel builds.
- **Array-backed filename sets** ([ocaml/dune#14487](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14487)): long-lived filename sets in watch mode now use sorted arrays (`Filename.Array.Set`) instead of balanced trees, reducing memory footprint in long-running watch sessions.
- **Removal of `.all-deps` transitive dependency files** ([ocaml/dune#14407](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14407)): transitive module dependency closures are now computed directly from `ocamldep`/`ocamlobjinfo` actions, removing intermediate materialised files and fixing a "Rule not found" error.
- **`cram`: boolean expressions in `runtest_alias`** ([ocaml/dune#14425](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14425)): cram tests now support boolean expressions in the `runtest_alias` field.
- **`dune install` error in package-managed workspaces** ([ocaml/dune#14427](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14427)): running `dune install` when dune package management is active now produces a clean user error rather than crashing.
- **[ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam) update handles file/directory renames** ([ocaml/opam#6915](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6915)): `OpamRepositoryBackend.get_diff` was rewritten to handle cases where a repository changes a file into a directory (or vice versa), closing [ocaml/opam#3830](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/3830) filed in 2019. A new `OpamFilename.Unix` module was added for cross-platform path handling.
- **[ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) `Misc` module relocated** ([ocaml/merlin#2059](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2059)): `Misc` and `Format_doc` moved from `merlin_utils` to `ocaml_utils` (`src/ocaml/utils/`), reducing diff against the upstream OCaml compiler.
- **[ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) ROADMAP.md restructured** ([ocaml/odoc#1421](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1421)): by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam), formalising four work streams: OxCaml support, new markup syntax, custom tags/plugins, and improved source rendering, plus Sherlodoc improvements.
- **[ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp) `pin-depends` in opam template** ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1603](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1603)): `merlin-lib` and `ocaml-index` pins added directly to `ocaml-lsp-server.opam.template` so `opam switch create .` works without manual pinning.
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) Wasm string conversion fix** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2230](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2230)): stubs for `length` and `intoCharCodeArray` added to the `string_ops` fallback, fixing `wasm:js-string` import resolution on engines lacking the JS String Builtins proposal.
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) Node.js 26 compatibility** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2234](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2234)): removed `--experimental-wasm-imported-strings` and `--experimental-wasm-stack-switching` flags, which Node.js 26 now enables by default.
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Six of ten tracked repositories had activity this week; [ocaml/ocamlfind](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind), [ocaml/opam-file-format](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-file-format), and [ocaml-dune/spawn](https://github.com/ocaml-dune/spawn) had no commits or open PRs.
- [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) was the most active repository with 50+ commits: dune 3.23.0 shipped on 2026-05-05 ([ocaml/dune#14212](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14212)) and a 3.23.1 regression cycle was immediately opened ([ocaml/dune#14441](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14441)) to address post-release issues in opam generation, OCaml version compatibility, and jsoo shared runtimes.
- [ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam) resolved a 7-year-old crash when repository directories changed shape ([ocaml/opam#6915](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6915), closing [ocaml/opam#3830](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/3830)), and opened multiple draft PRs advancing the repository layer overhaul ([ocaml/opam#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625)).
- [ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) updated its roadmap ([ocaml/odoc#1421](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1421)) to formalise OxCaml support, and addressed xref2 substitution failures against real-world OCaml 5.x packages ([ocaml/odoc#1429](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1429), [ocaml/odoc#1431](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1431)).
- [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) merged a compiler synchronisation refactor ([ocaml/merlin#2059](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2059)) and opened an architectural discussion on integrating ocamlgrep structural search ([ocaml/merlin#2063](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2063)).
- [ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp) adapted to Merlin's module rename ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1601](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1601)) and improved developer setup by encoding Merlin pin-depends in the opam template ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1603](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1603)).
- [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) merged four PRs covering Wasm correctness, variable coalescing, and Node.js 26 compatibility, while a dune 3.23 upgrade remains blocked on upstream regressions ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2231](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2231)).
group_summaries.activity_summary: Post-3.23.0 regression fixes in dune, opam repository layer modernisation, odoc OxCaml annotations review, and Wasm correctness work in js_of_ocaml.
group_summaries.activity:
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) 3.23.1 regression fixes**: [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) gated opam bound deduplication on `(lang dune 3.23)` ([ocaml/dune#14466](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14466), fixing [ocaml/dune#14436](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14436)); [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) fixed bootstrapping with `ocamlfind-secondary` on OCaml < 4.14 ([ocaml/dune#14443](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14443)); [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) fixed alphabetical sort corruption in shared jsoo runtime ordering ([ocaml/dune#14438](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14438)); both [ocaml/dune#14466](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14466) and [ocaml/dune#14443](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14443) were backported to the 3.23.1-rc ([ocaml/dune#14471](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14471), [ocaml/dune#14465](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14465)).
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) sandbox lifecycle refactored** ([ocaml/dune#14483](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14483)): `Sandbox.create`/`destroy` replaced by `Sandbox.with_`, with a `None` mode for lightweight non-sandboxed actions.
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) melange test coverage expansion**: [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) merged six melange test PRs ([ocaml/dune#14418](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14418), [ocaml/dune#14419](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14419), [ocaml/dune#14420](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14420), [ocaml/dune#14478](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14478), [ocaml/dune#14480](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14480), [ocaml/dune#14475](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14475)) covering nested subdirs, wrapped/unwrapped libraries, and melange-specific source preferences.
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) directory symlink resolution** ([ocaml/dune#13792](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13792)): [Ambre Austen Suhamy](https://github.com/ElectreAAS) landed support for resolving directory symlinks in fetched source archives, partially fixing [ocaml/dune#9873](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/9873).
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) menhir opam dep injection fix** ([ocaml/dune#14434](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14434)): menhir dependency now only injected for packages that actually use menhir, fixing [ocaml/dune#14428](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14428).
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) portable lock directories single solve** ([ocaml/dune#14458](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14458)): [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) opened a draft PR to implement a single SAT solve for all platforms, addressing [ocaml/dune#13645](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13645).
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) RPC debug improvements** ([ocaml/dune#14405](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14405)): [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) added in-flight RPC request tracking to SIGUSR2 debug dumps.
- **[ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam) autopin cleanup UX** ([ocaml/opam#6923](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6923)): draft PR by [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) to silently unpin automatically pinned packages when the user declines installation confirmation, complementing the regression test in [ocaml/opam#6922](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6922).
- **[ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam) repository load consistency** ([ocaml/opam#6924](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6924)): draft PR by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) to homogenise initial and diff-based repository loads, part of the tar.gz archive story ([ocaml/opam#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625)).
- **[ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam) documentation and CI improvements**: depopts filter note made bold in Manual ([ocaml/opam#6920](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6920), closing [ocaml/opam#5367](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/5367)); doc changes now trigger the documentation build job in CI ([ocaml/opam#6927](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6927), fixing [ocaml/opam#6810](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6810)).
- **[ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) `[@@zero_alloc]` OxCaml annotation PR** ([ocaml/odoc#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)): by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV), parsing `zero_alloc` attributes with `opt`, `strict`, and `arity <n>` payloads; under active review by [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd).
- **[ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) xref2 substitution bug fixes** ([ocaml/odoc#1430](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1430)): fix for assertion failure in `subst.ml` and uncaught exception in `apply_inner_substs` discovered via merlin-lib ([ocaml/odoc#1429](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1429)) and core_unix ([ocaml/odoc#1431](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1431)) build failures.
- **[ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) `bisect_ppx` dependency removed** ([ocaml/odoc#1428](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1428)) and OxCaml opam conflict constraint added ([ocaml/odoc#1432](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1432)) by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27).
- **[ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) saved-parts testing PR** ([ocaml/merlin#2060](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2060)) by [Thomas Refis](https://github.com/trefis) remains open, investigating a difference in how Merlin handles saved types versus the upstream typechecker.
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) variable coalescing fix** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2233](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2233)): corrected handling of unlabelled `break` targeting labelled non-iteration statements; CFG builder refactored with a new `visit_owning_stmt` to make context-ownership explicit.
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) Wasm lifecycle events PR** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2232](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2232)): [Jason Gross](https://github.com/JasonGross) proposed dispatching `wasmoocaml:loaded` and `wasmoocaml:error` CustomEvents on `globalThis` when async Wasm instantiation completes, ported from [ocaml-wasm/wasm_of_ocaml#143](https://github.com/ocaml-wasm/wasm_of_ocaml/issues/143).
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) dune 3.23 CI fix** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2236](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2236)): addressed newly enforced menhir lower bound in dune 3.23.
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **Merlin module rename → ocaml-lsp compatibility**: [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923)'s relocation of `Misc` from `Merlin_utils` to `Ocaml_utils` in [ocaml/merlin#2059](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2059) required an immediate compatibility fix in [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1601](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1601) (merged by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos)) to update `merlin_config.ml` references, and an opam `pin-depends` fix in [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1603](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1603) to ensure local development builds correctly track the Merlin development branch.
- **dune 3.23.0 regressions affecting downstream projects**: [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)'s dune 3.23 upgrade PR ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2231](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2231)) is blocked on [ocaml/dune#14441](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14441) (the 3.23.1 regression tracker) and [ocaml/dune#14438](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14438) (jsoo shared runtime ordering); the CI fix [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2236](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2236) addressed a separate menhir lower bound newly enforced by dune 3.23.
- **jsoo shared runtime fix by a cross-project contributor**: [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) — the primary [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) maintainer — authored the fix for alphabetical sort corruption in dune's shared jsoo runtime ([ocaml/dune#14438](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14438)), directly addressing a regression that blocked the downstream js_of_ocaml dune upgrade.
- **Relocatable compiler in dune connects to opam packaging**: [ocaml/dune#14357](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14357) (relocatable compiler support in `dune pkg`) relies on a relocatable OCaml compiler from David Allsopp's opam repository; [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) is also active in [ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) this week ([ocaml/odoc#1432](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1432)), adding an OxCaml opam conflict constraint.
- **odoc lock dir check in dune** ([ocaml/dune#14426](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14426)): a dune fix ensures that the lock directory is checked even when `lock_dev_tools` is disabled when invoking odoc, directly connecting dune package management with odoc invocation.
- **OxCaml support coordinated across tools**: [ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc)'s OxCaml tracking issue ([ocaml/odoc#1411](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1411)) managed by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) — who also cut the dune 3.23.0 release ([ocaml/dune#14212](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14212)) — reflects a coordinated cross-project effort to align the toolchain with OxCaml compiler features.
- **opam repository layer overhaul connects opam and dune package management**: the `OpamFilename.Unix` module introduced in [ocaml/opam#6915](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6915) and the ongoing `load_from_diff` consistency work ([ocaml/opam#6924](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6924)) are preparatory steps for tar.gz archive repository support ([ocaml/opam#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625)), which intersects with dune's package management layer that consumes opam repositories.
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **dune 3.23.1 regression tracker** ([ocaml/dune#14441](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14441)): [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) opened a dedicated tracker immediately after 3.23.0. Identified regressions include menhir spurious dep injection ([ocaml/dune#14428](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14428)), opam bound deduplication activating for pre-3.23 projects ([ocaml/dune#14436](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14436)), OCaml < 4.14 build breakage ([ocaml/dune#14443](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14443)), and jsoo shared runtime ordering corruption ([ocaml/dune#14438](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14438)).
- **Per-module inter-library dependency filtering** ([ocaml/dune#14472](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14472)): [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) restructured the long-running [ocaml/dune#14116](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14116) PR addressing [ocaml/dune#4572](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/4572). [Alain Frisch](https://github.com/alainfrisch) engaged on the trade-off between per-module fine-grained deps (larger graph, less recompilation) versus compiler-side `.cmx` stability checks.
- **ocamlgrep integration architecture** ([ocaml/merlin#2063](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2063)): [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) proposed integrating structural type-aware OCaml code search as a Merlin subcommand ([ocaml/merlin#2064](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2064)). [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) raised concerns about dependency layering and suggested deeper integration sharing Merlin's artifact caches. The discussion also surfaced the project's emerging position on AI-assisted contributions: authors must have full ownership of submitted code.
- **opam autopin cleanup UX** ([ocaml/opam#6923](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6923)): [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) and [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) agreed to silently unpin automatically pinned packages on declined installation, while leaving packages pinned after install *failure* since partial state may have been written.
- **`top-module` over-invalidation in dune** ([ocaml/dune#14477](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14477)): [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) filed a performance bug where `dune ocaml top-module` rebuilds artefacts when any library dependency's interface changes, even libraries only referenced from `.mli` which top-module drops; reproduction test in [ocaml/dune#14476](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14476).
- **[ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) `[@@zero_alloc]` representation** ([ocaml/odoc#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)): [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) and [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) debated a record type `{ opt; strict; arity }` vs. a variant representation, and whether to silently ignore invalid payloads (given the OxCaml compiler will catch them at source).
- **ocaml-lsp opam downgrade on OCaml 4.14** ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1604](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1604)): [Török Edwin](https://github.com/edwintorok) reported that `opam upgrade` attempts to downgrade `ocaml-lsp-server` from `1.21.0-4.14` to `1.17.0` due to version resolution interplay with the `lsp` sub-library split.
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **OxCaml support across the toolchain**: [ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) is formalising OxCaml feature support via a tracking issue ([ocaml/odoc#1411](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1411)), roadmap update ([ocaml/odoc#1421](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1421)), new annotation PR ([ocaml/odoc#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)), and opam conflict constraints ([ocaml/odoc#1432](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1432)); dune's relocatable compiler work ([ocaml/dune#14357](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14357)) provides a foundation for this via improved package management.
- **Per-module dependency precision in dune**: the multi-PR effort ([ocaml/dune#14472](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14472), [ocaml/dune#14473](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14473), [ocaml/dune#14474](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14474)) by [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) to filter compile-rule dependencies per module using `ocamldep` output continues to grow in scope, with discussion from [Alain Frisch](https://github.com/alainfrisch) on performance implications for large monorepos.
- **Build engine resource management in dune**: multiple merged PRs this week ([ocaml/dune#14464](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14464), [ocaml/dune#14483](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14483), [ocaml/dune#14487](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14487)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) reflect a sustained drive to reduce memory pressure, file descriptor exhaustion, and disk usage in long-running watch sessions.
- **Tight Merlin–LSP coupling requiring coordinated tracking**: the need for both a compatibility patch ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1601](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1601)) and an opam pin change ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1603](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1603)) in the same week illustrates a recurring pattern where ocaml-lsp must track Merlin's `main` branch closely between releases, with developer onboarding breakage as a consequence ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1602](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1602)).
- **Post-release regression discipline in dune**: the 3.23.1 cycle ([ocaml/dune#14441](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14441)) demonstrates a maturing practice of gating behaviour changes on `(lang dune X.Y)` version, with [ocaml/dune#14466](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14466) and [ocaml/dune#14453](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14453) retroactively applying version guards to previously unguarded changes.
- **Wasm correctness and runtime compatibility in js_of_ocaml**: fixes for string conversion fallbacks ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2230](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2230)), variable coalescing ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2233](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2233)), and a new lifecycle events proposal ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2232](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2232)) reflect continued broadening of Wasm target support alongside ongoing native effects ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2189](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2189)) and WASI runtime work ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831)).
- **opam repository layer overhaul progressing steadily**: the coordinated set of PRs ([ocaml/opam#6915](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6915), [ocaml/opam#6924](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6924), [ocaml/opam#6929](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6929), [ocaml/opam#6871](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6871)) are all preparatory for tar.gz archive support as a first-class repository format ([ocaml/opam#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625)).

group_summaries.brief_summary: Merlin consolidated into oxcaml/oxcaml monorepo, ikinds enabled by default, and new opam packages added for dune, odoc, and extlib.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: ikinds on by default, Patricia trees with GADTs, AVX/VEX encoding improvements, Merlin subtree merged, extlib/dune/odoc opam packages added.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **ikinds enabled by default** in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml): the `-ikinds` flag replaced by `-no-ikinds`; prerequisite ikind substitution fix also merged ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5998](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5998), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5999](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5999)) by [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) and [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923)
- **Merlin added as a git subtree** at `external/merlin/` in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml), bringing full Merlin history into the monorepo with CI integration ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5568](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5568)) by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923)
- **Patricia trees use GADTs to encode invariants**: `Empty` can only appear at toplevel, enabling allocation-free `Set` operations; `Set.union` code size halved ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5899](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5899)) by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs)
- **Shorter AVX/VEX encodings** in the x86 binary emitter: 2-byte VEX prefix support ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6012](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6012)), shorter `vmovq`/`movapd`/`movupd` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6022](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6022)), and 2-byte VEX for YMM reg-reg moves ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6033](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6033)) by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon)
- **Allow implicit kind redeclaration**: nested `[@@@implicit_kind]` annotations differing from outer declaration now permitted ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5980](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5980)) by [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js)
- **Doc comments on kind declarations** preserved: bug that silently dropped `(** ... *)` documentation on kind declarations fixed ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5987](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5987)) by [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin)
- **Better error messages for `Invalid_native_repr_for_primitive`**: now include a `Hint:` pointing to `[@unpacked]` for product layouts in C stub arguments ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5985](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5985)) by [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4)
- **Fix hidden dependency cache checking**: `-H` warning behavior now consistent regardless of cache state and lookup order ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6020](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6020)) by [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs)
- **Fix modes bug in untypeast/pprintast roundtrip**: modalities on `val`/`external` items now correctly restored ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5986](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5986)) by [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin)
- **Syntactic sugar for fexpr `switch` branches** inlining `goto` handler code directly ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5474](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5474)) by [Keryan Didier](https://github.com/Keryan-dev)
- **fexpr: Display stub information** for code symbols in Flambda2 IR output ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6004](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6004)) by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp)
- **New `extlib.1.8.0+ox`** package enabling extlib to build under OxCaml ([oxcaml/opam-repository#44](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/44))
- **New `dune.3.22.2+ox`** and **`odoc.3.2.0+ox`** packages (plus odoc-driver, odoc-md, odoc-parser, sherlodoc variants), resolving a CI breakage since dune 3.22 is required for OxCaml detection by latest odoc ([oxcaml/opam-repository#39](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/39))
- **New `opam-core.2.5.1+ox`** and **`opam-format.2.5.1+ox`** packages aligned with the upstream opam 2.5.1 release ([oxcaml/opam-repository#41](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/41))
- **New `oxcaml.action-required`** virtual package providing actionable error messages for users missing the overlay repository ([oxcaml/opam-repository#40](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/40))
- **oxcaml/merlin repository deprecated**: contents removed and README redirected to [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) as canonical home ([oxcaml/merlin#240](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/240)) by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923)
group_summaries.group_overview:
- [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) was highly active with 24 contributors: Merlin integrated as a subtree ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5568](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5568)), ikinds enabled by default ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5999](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5999)), and sustained 5.4 merge preparation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5958](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5958))
- [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository) added five new or updated packages (extlib, dune 3.22.2, odoc 3.2.0, opam-core/format 2.5.1), removed deprecated odoc 3.1.0+ox patches ([oxcaml/opam-repository#43](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/43)), and added onboarding infrastructure ([oxcaml/opam-repository#40](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/40))
- [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) was deprecated as a standalone repository ([oxcaml/merlin#240](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/240)); in-flight PRs redirected to [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml)
- [oxcaml/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/js_of_ocaml) and [janestreet/opam-repository](https://github.com/janestreet/opam-repository) had no recorded activity this week
group_summaries.activity_summary: 5.4 merge preparation, x86 binary emitter correctness, layout-poly advances, metaprogramming unloading, and SSA IR all progressed this week.
group_summaries.activity:
- **5.4 merge preparation** advanced via [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js)'s PRs for DWARF SLEB128 encoding ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6021](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6021)), frame pointer preservation at noalloc C calls ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6014](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6014)), and the staging integration PR ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5958](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5958)); effects typechecking fix opened by [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6001](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6001))
- **x86 binary emitter correctness**: [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) opened NOP encoding/alignment ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6028](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6028), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6029](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6029)), monotone backwards jumps ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6025](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6025)), shorter 16-bit immediate arithmetic ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6027](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6027)), and removal of a jump distance heuristic ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6024](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6024))
- **Metaprogramming compilation unit unloading** PR opened by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5960](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5960)), introducing mark/sweep GC for unloadable CUs with code blocks as heap objects
- **SSA intermediate representation** PR opened by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5810](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5810)), a new backend pass between Cmm and CFG with a GADT-typed graph API and a `Cfg_compare` validator
- **Layout polymorphism** advances: `poly_` printing in value descriptions ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6038](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6038)) by [Andrej Ivašković](https://github.com/Dreian), layout-poly static data via callbacks ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6049](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6049)) and `Compilenv` split ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6030](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6030)) by [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr), persistent-module staticity via CMI modalities ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6041](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6041)) by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn), and layout variables in product kind annotations ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6026](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6026))
- **Merlin build rules and import script** follow-ups after the subtree merge: [oxcaml/oxcaml#6042](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6042) and [oxcaml/oxcaml#6034](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6034) by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923)
- **Reaper bug fixes** for set-of-closures rewriting ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6005](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6005)) and draft inlining improvement ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5991](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5991)) by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs)
- **`Cfg_quick_hash` module** extracted from `Cfg_merge_blocks` for passes requiring equality up to renaming ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5788](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5788)) by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc)
- **Record representation cleanup**: `Record_boxed` no longer stores an array of sorts ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6002](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6002)); follow-up refactoring opened ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6035](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6035)) by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa)
- **`-open-cmi` new compiler flag** to open a module signature directly from a `.cmi` file ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6013](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6013)) by [Jakub Staroń](https://github.com/staronj)
- **Fiber ID recording for magic-trace** via PTWRITE instruction when Intel PT is active ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6032](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6032)) by [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat)
- **Reaper points-to analysis refactoring** merged: more principled access functions for usages and sources ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5938](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5938)) by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp)
- **`misc.ml` synchronized** in [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) to match compiler layout before deprecation ([oxcaml/merlin#238](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/238)) by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923)
- **ocamlbuild Dune compatibility patch** ([oxcaml/opam-repository#21](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/21)) remains under review; [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) suggested using `extra-source` and a `with-dune-package-management` opam variable instead
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **Merlin monorepo consolidation** links [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) and [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml): the standalone Merlin repo was deprecated ([oxcaml/merlin#240](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/240)) after its full history was merged as a git subtree ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5568](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5568)); two in-flight PRs from [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) ([oxcaml/merlin#223](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/223), [oxcaml/merlin#205](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/205)) were redirected to [oxcaml/oxcaml#6045](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6045) and [oxcaml/oxcaml#6044](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6044)
- **opam-repository dune 3.22.2+ox** ([oxcaml/opam-repository#39](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/39)) is a prerequisite for the latest odoc's OxCaml detection in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) CI — the upgrade was required to fix a CI breakage
- **5.4 merge preparation** in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) includes DWARF/debug fixes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6021](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6021)) that parallel upstream tracking at [ocaml/ocamlbuild#362](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlbuild/issues/362) discussed in [oxcaml/opam-repository#21](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/21)
- **`opam-core.2.5.1+ox`** patch updated ([oxcaml/opam-repository#41](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/41)) to support `oxcaml-compiler 5.2.0minus31`, tracking compiler version bumps in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml)
- **Upstream odoc patches eliminated**: removal of odoc 3.1.0+ox packages ([oxcaml/opam-repository#43](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/43)) tracks the upstream [ocaml/merlin#2059](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2059) synchronization that also landed in [oxcaml/merlin#238](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/238)
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) and [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) discussed correctness and performance trade-offs of replacing `Map` with `Hashtable` in the mode solver ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5962](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5962)), noting a 3% regression; [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) opened a separate fix using a total structural order for solver morph map keys ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6047](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6047))
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) and [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) discussed the vendored vs. explicit Merlin build workflow in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5568](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5568), covering how `dune-workspace` files interact with the boot/runtime_stdlib/main workspaces and why the boot context uses the `default` name to keep editor tooling working
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) raised technical concerns on [oxcaml/opam-repository#21](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/21) about the `ocamlbuild` Dune compatibility patch, suggesting simpler approaches using `extra-source` with upstream commits ([ocaml/ocamlbuild#362](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlbuild/issues/362), [ocaml/ocamlbuild#327](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlbuild/issues/327)) and a `with-dune-package-management` opam variable
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js)'s DWARF SLEB128 analysis in [oxcaml/oxcaml#6021](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6021) explains why frame-pointer builds were unaffected (offset 56 fits in a single SLEB128 byte, offset 72 without frame pointers does not)
- [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) and [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) debated whether `borrow_` aliasing behavior should be documented given it is an implementation artifact rather than a designed feature ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5928](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5928))
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) provided closure comments on [oxcaml/merlin#223](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/223) and [oxcaml/merlin#205](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/205) directing contributors to re-open work in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml)
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Merlin monorepo integration** is becoming a sustained project: the subtree merge ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5568](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5568)) triggered immediate follow-ups for build rules ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6042](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6042)), import script updates ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6034](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6034)), CI improvements ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6051](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6051)), and migration of existing PRs ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6044](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6044), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6045](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6045)), with deprecation of the standalone [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) repo ([oxcaml/merlin#240](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/240))
- **5.4 merge preparation is intensifying**: multiple PRs ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6021](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6021), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6014](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6014), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5989](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5989), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5988](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5988), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5972](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5972), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6015](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6015)) target the 5.4 branch with [oxcaml/oxcaml#5958](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5958) as the staging integration PR
- **Binary emitter systematic review**: [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) landed three VEX encoding PRs and opened five more in one week, indicating a methodical audit of x86 emitter correctness covering NOP padding, jump encoding, and prefix selection ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6012](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6012), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6022](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6022), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6033](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6033), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6028](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6028), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6029](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6029))
- **Layout polymorphism advancing on multiple fronts simultaneously**: `val poly_` printing ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6038](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6038)), layout variables in product kinds ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6026](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6026)), `Compilenv` split ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6030](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6030)), static data via callbacks ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6049](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6049)), and foundational `any`-kind PRs ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5461](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5461), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5462](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5462))
- **Upstream patch absorption in opam-repository**: removal of odoc 3.1.0+ox packages ([oxcaml/opam-repository#43](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/43)) and closure of the odoc-parser compatibility PR ([oxcaml/opam-repository#22](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/22)) reflect a pattern where OxCaml-specific patches are upstreamed, allowing the overlay to shift from custom patches to plain version constraints
- **Growing opam ecosystem coverage**: addition of `extlib.1.8.0+ox` ([oxcaml/opam-repository#44](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/44)) alongside `dune.3.22.2+ox`, `odoc.3.2.0+ox`, and `opam-core/format.2.5.1+ox` ([oxcaml/opam-repository#39](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/39), [oxcaml/opam-repository#41](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/41)) indicates continued broadening of OxCaml-compatible package coverage

group_summaries.brief_summary: Quiet week in ocaml/ocaml: stdlib improvements, typechecker refactoring, Unix bug fixes, and CI hardening; ocaml/RFCs had no activity.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Seq.repeat made constant-space via a cyclic closure/cons-cell pair, eliminating per-element allocation.
group_summaries.new_features: - [ocaml/ocaml#14783](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14783) by [Jeremy Yallop](https://github.com/yallop): **`Seq.repeat` is now constant-space**, implemented with a single cyclic closure and cons cell rather than allocating new cells per element, reducing memory usage for long sequences.
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Activity this week was entirely in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml); [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) had no recorded activity.
- Seven PRs were merged into trunk spanning stdlib optimisation ([ocaml/ocaml#14783](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14783)), typechecker refactoring ([ocaml/ocaml#14800](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14800), [ocaml/ocaml#14795](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14795)), Unix library thread-safety and Windows correctness ([ocaml/ocaml#14715](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14715), [ocaml/ocaml#14788](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14788)), ocamldoc ([ocaml/ocaml#14794](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14794)), and CI hardening ([ocaml/ocaml#14787](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14787), [ocaml/ocaml#14676](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14676)).
- Notable open work includes a draft new GC pacing algorithm ([ocaml/ocaml#14796](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14796)) and native atomic fetch-add across multiple backends ([ocaml/ocaml#14575](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14575)).
- Discussions touched on runtime event flooding, Dynarray concurrency documentation, and a stdlib proposal for a non-empty list type.
group_summaries.activity_summary: Seven PRs merged covering typechecker global-state removal, Unix thread-safety fixes, Windows error mapping, ocamldoc, and CI security.
group_summaries.activity:
- [ocaml/ocaml#14800](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14800) by [Matthew Marques](https://github.com/mattm3465): Localized `nondep_type_rec` memoisation state into a new `Nondep_scope` module in `typing/ctype.ml`, replacing module-global hash tables with per-call scope instances; reviewed by [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) and [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche).
- [ocaml/ocaml#14715](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14715) by [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA): Fixed null-pointer dereferences and a missing `HAVE_GETLOGIN_R` define in several POSIX thread-safe functions in libunix (`getgrnam_r`, `getgrgid_r`, `getpwnam_r`, `getpwuid_r`, `getlogin_r`); cherry-picked to 5.5 by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron).
- [ocaml/ocaml#14795](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14795) by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron): Removed dead mutual recursion in `typing/env.ml` that had been present since OCaml 4.00, splitting `store_{type,value,...}` / `module_components` into independent definitions.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14794](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14794) by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron): Fixed the `ocamldoc` `is_function` predicate to recognise modular explicit (module-dependent) function types.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14788](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14788) by [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb): Mapped Win32 `ERROR_DIRECTORY` (267) to `ENOTDIR` instead of `EUNKNOWNERR(-267)`, fixing [ocaml/ocaml#14767](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14767) so that `Unix.opendir` on a file raises the expected error on Windows.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14787](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14787) by [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole): Fixed a wrong `GITHUB_TOKEN` variable name not propagated to `check-changes-updated.sh` and clarified CI comments from [ocaml/ocaml#14676](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14676).
- [ocaml/ocaml#14676](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14676) by [Austin Theriault](https://github.com/ajbt200128): Prevented shell injection in GitHub Actions by placing GHA expression variables into environment variables rather than expanding them directly in `run` scripts.
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- [ocaml/ocaml#14791](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14791) opened by [nicki](https://github.com/curche): Proposes emitting `EV_DOMAIN_CONDITION_WAIT` only in the instrumented runtime after observing event flooding in large Infer runs (46K events vs. ~750 for other phases at `-j14`); [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) questioned whether the event should be removed entirely, noting that `Condition.wait` is a user-level action rather than a runtime action.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14798](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14798) by [Nathan Taylor](https://github.com/dijkstracula): Proposes rewording the `Dynarray` concurrency documentation to be more precise; [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) confirmed only resizing operations are truly unsafe, noting the broader work of documenting concurrent usage for all stdlib mutable structures remains pending.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14261](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/discussions/14261) by [Mathieu Barbin](https://github.com/mbarbin): Ongoing discussion on adding a `Nonempty_list.t` type to the stdlib (using the `(::)` constructor trick for list-syntax compatibility) purely for interoperability between third-party libraries, without adding further API beyond the type definition.
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Removal of global mutable state from the typechecker**: [ocaml/ocaml#14800](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14800) (`Nondep_scope`) and [ocaml/ocaml#14795](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14795) continue a pattern of converting module-level hash tables and dead mutual recursions into locally-scoped values, improving thread-safety and modularity of compiler internals.
- **Cross-platform Unix correctness**: [ocaml/ocaml#14715](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14715) and [ocaml/ocaml#14788](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14788) both address platform-specific bugs in the Unix library, reflecting sustained effort to align POSIX bindings across Linux, macOS, and Windows.
- **Draft GC pacing algorithm**: [ocaml/ocaml#14796](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14796) by [Damien Doligez](https://github.com/damiendoligez) introduces a new GC pacing algorithm (686 additions across 17 files) as an early-stage draft, signalling upcoming changes to GC throughput tuning.
- **Native atomic fetch-add across backends**: [ocaml/ocaml#14575](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14575) by [Zane Hambly](https://github.com/Zaneham) continues review for emitting native `Iatomic_fetch_add` on amd64, arm64, riscv, and power, advancing multicore performance.

group_summaries.brief_summary: Quiet week across the multicore group; eio saw a design discussion on adding Eio.Promise.map.
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Activity this week was limited to [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio); all other repositories (domainslib, ocaml-uring, meio, saturn, eio-trace, picos, kcas, ocaml-iomux) had no recorded activity.
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) opened [ocaml-multicore/eio#838](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/838) proposing an `Eio.Promise.map` function to enable functional-style promise composition without requiring a switch.
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) responded with a detailed analysis of the design constraints, explaining why a straightforward implementation is non-trivial given Eio's concurrency safety guarantees.
group_summaries.activity_summary: Patrick Ferris proposed Eio.Promise.map in eio#838, prompting a design discussion on promise and fiber semantics.
group_summaries.activity:
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) opened [ocaml-multicore/eio#838](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/838) requesting an `Eio.Promise.map` function, noting that the existing workaround (`Fiber.fork ~sw`) requires a switch and undermines the switchless promise API design.
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) outlined three obstacles in [ocaml-multicore/eio#838](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/838): promise resolution intentionally runs no user code (making it safe from signal handlers), so the resolving domain cannot execute the mapping function; delegating to the awaiting domain still requires a switch; and lazy evaluation introduces locking concerns when multiple domains await simultaneously.
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) concluded in [ocaml-multicore/eio#838](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/838) that his specific use case — a non-yielding, immediate transformation analogous to `Option.get` — can accept a switch, resolving the immediate need.
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- [ocaml-multicore/eio#838](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/838): [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) explained that `Condition.register_immediate` exists for immediate callbacks but is intended for IO library integration rather than general use, clarifying where `Eio.Promise.map` would fit in the broader API surface.
- The exchange in [ocaml-multicore/eio#838](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/838) surfaces a recurring tension in Eio's design: ergonomic functional composition (e.g. `Promise.map`, `Promise.bind`) versus the deliberate constraint that promise resolution must not execute arbitrary user code.
group_summaries.emerging_trends: - The proposal in [ocaml-multicore/eio#838](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/838) reflects continued interest in making Eio's promise primitives composable in a functional style, similar to patterns common in `Lwt` or `Async`, while preserving Eio's stronger concurrency-safety guarantees.

weekly_summary.brief_summary: Dune 3.23.0 released with sandboxing by default; OxCaml Merlin consolidated into monorepo and ikinds enabled by default across 24 contributors.
weekly_summary.new_features_summary: Dune 3.23.0 ships sandboxed rules, relocatable compiler, RPC flush-file-watcher; ikinds on by default in OxCaml; Seq.repeat constant-space; OCaml <4.11 packages archived.
weekly_summary.new_features:
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) 3.23.0 released** ([ocaml/dune#14212](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14212)) by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder): user rules are now **sandboxed by default**, `compile_commands.json` generated for C/C++ foreign stubs, shared jsoo runtimes, hardlink sandboxing on Windows, and `dune clean` target arguments
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Relocatable compiler in `dune pkg`** ([ocaml/dune#14357](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14357)): dune now detects and uses the relocatable OCaml compiler from David Allsopp's opam repository, treating the compiler as a cacheable package and closing [ocaml/dune#13229](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13229) and [ocaml/dune#14012](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14012)
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **`dune rpc flush-file-watcher` command** ([ocaml/dune#14467](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14467)): new RPC procedure allowing clients to synchronise with the file watcher before issuing build requests
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **RPC connection status in build status line** ([ocaml/dune#14424](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14424)): shows a `[rpc N]` section; `Console.Status_line` gained `add_section`/`remove_section` APIs for composable persistent fragments
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Live sandbox throttle** ([ocaml/dune#14464](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14464)): a global limit of 250 slots prevents unbounded concurrent sandbox directory creation
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Array-backed filename sets** ([ocaml/dune#14487](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14487)): long-lived filename sets in watch mode now use sorted arrays (`Filename.Array.Set`) instead of balanced trees, reducing memory footprint in long-running watch sessions
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Removal of `.all-deps` transitive dependency files** ([ocaml/dune#14407](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14407)): transitive module dependency closures now computed directly from `ocamldep`/`ocamlobjinfo` actions, fixing a "Rule not found" error
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **`cram` boolean expressions in `runtest_alias`** ([ocaml/dune#14425](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14425)): cram tests now support boolean expressions in the `runtest_alias` field
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **[ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam) update handles file/directory renames** ([ocaml/opam#6915](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6915)): `OpamRepositoryBackend.get_diff` rewritten to handle cases where a repository changes a file into a directory (or vice versa), closing [ocaml/opam#3830](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/3830) filed in 2019
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **[ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) `Misc` module relocated** ([ocaml/merlin#2059](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2059)): `Misc` and `Format_doc` moved from `merlin_utils` to `ocaml_utils`, reducing diff against the upstream OCaml compiler
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **[ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp) `pin-depends` in opam template** ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1603](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1603)): `merlin-lib` and `ocaml-index` pins added directly to `ocaml-lsp-server.opam.template` so `opam switch create .` works without manual pinning
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) Wasm string conversion fix** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2230](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2230)): stubs for `length` and `intoCharCodeArray` added to the `string_ops` fallback, fixing `wasm:js-string` import resolution on engines lacking the JS String Builtins proposal
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) Node.js 26 compatibility** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2234](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2234)): removed `--experimental-wasm-imported-strings` and `--experimental-wasm-stack-switching` flags, which Node.js 26 now enables by default
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **[ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) ROADMAP.md restructured** ([ocaml/odoc#1421](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1421)) by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam), formalising four work streams: OxCaml support, new markup syntax, custom tags/plugins, and improved source rendering
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **ikinds enabled by default** in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml): the `-ikinds` flag replaced by `-no-ikinds`; prerequisite ikind substitution fix also merged ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5998](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5998), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5999](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5999)) by [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) and [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Merlin added as a git subtree** at `external/merlin/` in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml), bringing full Merlin history into the monorepo with CI integration ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5568](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5568)) by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Patricia trees use GADTs to encode invariants**: `Empty` can only appear at toplevel, enabling allocation-free `Set` operations; `Set.union` code size halved ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5899](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5899)) by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Shorter AVX/VEX encodings** in the x86 binary emitter: 2-byte VEX prefix support ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6012](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6012)), shorter `vmovq`/`movapd`/`movupd` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6022](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6022)), and 2-byte VEX for YMM reg-reg moves ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6033](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6033)) by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Allow implicit kind redeclaration**: nested `[@@@implicit_kind]` annotations differing from outer declaration now permitted ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5980](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5980)) by [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Doc comments on kind declarations** preserved: bug that silently dropped `(** ... *)` documentation on kind declarations fixed ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5987](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5987)) by [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Better error messages for `Invalid_native_repr_for_primitive`**: now include a `Hint:` pointing to `[@unpacked]` for product layouts in C stub arguments ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5985](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5985)) by [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **New `extlib.1.8.0+ox`** package enabling extlib to build under OxCaml ([oxcaml/opam-repository#44](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/44))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **New `dune.3.22.2+ox`** and **`odoc.3.2.0+ox`** packages (plus odoc-driver, odoc-md, odoc-parser, sherlodoc variants), resolving a CI breakage ([oxcaml/opam-repository#39](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/39))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **New `oxcaml.action-required`** virtual package providing actionable error messages for users missing the overlay repository ([oxcaml/opam-repository#40](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/40))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **oxcaml/merlin repository deprecated**: contents removed and README redirected to [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) as canonical home ([oxcaml/merlin#240](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/240)) by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923)
- __RUMINANT:core__ **`Seq.repeat` is now constant-space** ([ocaml/ocaml#14783](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14783)) by [Jeremy Yallop](https://github.com/yallop): implemented with a single cyclic closure and cons cell rather than allocating new cells per element
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **OCaml compiler archival cutoff raised to 4.11**: [ocaml/opam-repository#29848](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29848) removed 1,132 package definition files (53,000+ lines) for compilers below 4.11
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **dune 3.23.0** published on [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29844](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29844)) with release announcement on ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3637](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3637))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **miou 0.6.0** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29855](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29855)) adds a `Miou.Trace` module with runtime event integration and `Miou.take` for consuming tasks from `Miou.orphans`
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **vif 0.0.1~beta3** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29876](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29876)) adds UNIX domain socket listener support, graceful server shutdown, and an OPTIONS route method
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Camlp5 8.05.00** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29874](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29874)) adds OCaml 5.5.0 syntax support; bugfix **8.04.01** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29884](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29884)) corrects labeled tuple handling
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **ppx_deriving_jsonschema 0.0.7** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29819](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29819)) adds `[@@jsonschema.compact_variants]`, `[@@jsonschema.format]`, `[@@jsonschema.description]`, `[@jsonschema.default]`, and `[@jsonschema.attrs]` attributes, making `option` types nullable
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **art 0.4.0** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29873](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29873)) adds the `Art.map` function to the Adaptive Radix Tree library
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **AbsInt** added to the ocaml.org industrial users page ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3647](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3647)), documenting their use of OCaml in the Astrée static analyser and CompCert compiler
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ Draft release changelog pages added to ocaml.org for **dune-release 2.2.1**, **mdx 2.5.2**, **merlin v5.7.0-504/v5.7.1-504/v5.7-505~preview**, **ocaml-lsp 1.26.0-5.5~preview**, **ocamlformat 0.29.0**, **OCaml 5.5.0~alpha2/~beta1**, **odoc 3.2.0**, **opam-publish 3.0.0**, and **utop 2.17.0** via [ocaml/ocaml.org#3641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3641)
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **`CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`** formally added to opam-repository ([ocaml/opam-repository#29850](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29850)), adopting the OCaml Code of Conduct

-**Shorter AVX/VEX encodings** in the x86 binary emitter: 2-byte VEX prefix support ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6012](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6012)), shorter `vmovq`/`movapd`/`movupd` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6022](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6022)), and 2-byte VEX for YMM reg-reg moves ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6033](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6033)) by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon)

-**Allow implicit kind redeclaration**: nested `[@@@implicit_kind]` annotations differing from outer declaration now permitted ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5980](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5980)) by [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js)

-**Doc comments on kind declarations** preserved: bug that silently dropped `(** ... *)` documentation on kind declarations fixed ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5987](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5987)) by [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin)

-**Better error messages for `Invalid_native_repr_for_primitive`**: now include a `Hint:` pointing to `[@unpacked]` for product layouts in C stub arguments ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5985](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5985)) by [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4)
-**New `extlib.1.8.0+ox`** package enabling extlib to build under OxCaml ([oxcaml/opam-repository#44](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/44))

-**New `dune.3.22.2+ox`** and **`odoc.3.2.0+ox`** packages (plus odoc-driver, odoc-md, odoc-parser, sherlodoc variants), resolving a CI breakage ([oxcaml/opam-repository#39](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/39))

-**New `oxcaml.action-required`** virtual package providing actionable error messages for users missing the overlay repository ([oxcaml/opam-repository#40](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/40))

-**oxcaml/merlin repository deprecated**: contents removed and README redirected to [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) as canonical home ([oxcaml/merlin#240](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/240)) by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923)

- __RUMINANT:tools__ [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) was the most active repository with 50+ commits: **dune 3.23.0** shipped on 2026-05-05 ([ocaml/dune#14212](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14212)) and a **3.23.1 regression cycle** was immediately opened ([ocaml/dune#14441](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14441)) to address post-release issues in opam generation, OCaml version compatibility, and jsoo shared runtimes
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) was highly active with **24 contributors**: **Merlin integrated as a subtree** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5568](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5568)), **ikinds enabled by default** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5999](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5999)), and sustained 5.4 merge preparation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5958](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5958)); [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) was deprecated as a standalone repository ([oxcaml/merlin#240](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/240)) after its history was absorbed into the monorepo
- __RUMINANT:core__ [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) merged **7 PRs** covering `Seq.repeat` constant-space optimization ([ocaml/ocaml#14783](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14783)), typechecker global-state removal ([ocaml/ocaml#14800](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14800), [ocaml/ocaml#14795](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14795)), Unix thread-safety and Windows correctness fixes ([ocaml/ocaml#14715](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14715), [ocaml/ocaml#14788](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14788)), and CI hardening ([ocaml/ocaml#14787](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14787), [ocaml/ocaml#14676](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14676))

- __RUMINANT:multicore__ Activity across the multicore group was minimal; [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) saw a design discussion on `Eio.Promise.map` ([ocaml-multicore/eio#838](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/838)) with [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) outlining concurrency-safety constraints; all other repositories had no recorded activity
