weekly_summary.activity_summary: Post-3.23.0 regression fixes in dune, opam repository layer modernisation, OxCaml layout polymorphism and binary emitter advances, and odoc OxCaml annotations review.
weekly_summary.activity:
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Dune 3.23.1 regression fixes**: [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) gated opam bound deduplication on `(lang dune 3.23)` ([ocaml/dune#14466](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14466), fixing [ocaml/dune#14436](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14436)); [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) fixed bootstrapping with `ocamlfind-secondary` on OCaml < 4.14 ([ocaml/dune#14443](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14443)); [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) fixed alphabetical sort corruption in shared jsoo runtime ordering ([ocaml/dune#14438](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14438)); both backported to the 3.23.1-rc ([ocaml/dune#14471](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14471), [ocaml/dune#14465](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14465))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Dune sandbox lifecycle refactored** ([ocaml/dune#14483](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14483)): `Sandbox.create`/`destroy` replaced by `Sandbox.with_`, with a `None` mode for lightweight non-sandboxed actions
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Melange test coverage expansion**: [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) merged six melange test PRs ([ocaml/dune#14418](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14418), [ocaml/dune#14419](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14419), [ocaml/dune#14420](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14420), [ocaml/dune#14478](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14478), [ocaml/dune#14480](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14480), [ocaml/dune#14475](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14475)) covering nested subdirs, wrapped/unwrapped libraries, and melange-specific source preferences
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Directory symlink resolution in dune** ([ocaml/dune#13792](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13792)): [Ambre Austen Suhamy](https://github.com/ElectreAAS) landed support for resolving directory symlinks in fetched source archives, partially fixing [ocaml/dune#9873](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/9873)
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Menhir opam dep injection fix** ([ocaml/dune#14434](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14434)): menhir dependency now only injected for packages that actually use menhir, fixing [ocaml/dune#14428](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14428)
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Portable lock directories single solve** ([ocaml/dune#14458](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14458)): [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) opened a draft PR to implement a single SAT solve for all platforms, addressing [ocaml/dune#13645](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13645)
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **RPC debug improvements** ([ocaml/dune#14405](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14405)): [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) added in-flight RPC request tracking to SIGUSR2 debug dumps
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Opam autopin cleanup UX** ([ocaml/opam#6923](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6923)): draft PR by [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) to silently unpin automatically pinned packages when the user declines installation confirmation
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Opam repository load consistency** ([ocaml/opam#6924](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6924)): draft PR by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) to homogenise initial and diff-based repository loads, part of the tar.gz archive story ([ocaml/opam#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **odoc `[@@zero_alloc]` OxCaml annotation PR** ([ocaml/odoc#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)): by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV), parsing `zero_alloc` attributes with `opt`, `strict`, and `arity <n>` payloads; under active review by [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd)
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **odoc xref2 substitution bug fixes** ([ocaml/odoc#1430](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1430)): fix for assertion failure in `subst.ml` and uncaught exception in `apply_inner_substs` discovered via merlin-lib ([ocaml/odoc#1429](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1429)) and core_unix ([ocaml/odoc#1431](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1431)) build failures
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **js_of_ocaml variable coalescing fix** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2233](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2233)): corrected handling of unlabelled `break` targeting labelled non-iteration statements; CFG builder refactored with a new `visit_owning_stmt` to make context-ownership explicit
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **js_of_ocaml Wasm lifecycle events PR** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2232](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2232)): [Jason Gross](https://github.com/JasonGross) proposed dispatching `wasmoocaml:loaded` and `wasmoocaml:error` CustomEvents on `globalThis` when async Wasm instantiation completes
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **5.4 merge preparation** advanced via [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js)'s PRs for DWARF SLEB128 encoding ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6021](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6021)), frame pointer preservation at noalloc C calls ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6014](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6014)), and the staging integration PR ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5958](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5958))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **x86 binary emitter correctness**: [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) opened NOP encoding/alignment ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6028](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6028), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6029](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6029)), monotone backwards jumps ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6025](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6025)), shorter 16-bit immediate arithmetic ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6027](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6027)), and removal of a jump distance heuristic ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6024](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6024))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Metaprogramming compilation unit unloading** PR opened by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5960](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5960)), introducing mark/sweep GC for unloadable CUs with code blocks as heap objects
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **SSA intermediate representation** PR opened by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5810](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5810)), a new backend pass between Cmm and CFG with a GADT-typed graph API and a `Cfg_compare` validator
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Layout polymorphism** advances: `poly_` printing in value descriptions ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6038](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6038)) by [Andrej Ivašković](https://github.com/Dreian), layout-poly static data via callbacks ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6049](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6049)) and `Compilenv` split ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6030](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6030)) by [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr), persistent-module staticity via CMI modalities ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6041](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6041)) by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Merlin build rules and import script** follow-ups after the subtree merge: [oxcaml/oxcaml#6042](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6042) and [oxcaml/oxcaml#6034](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6034) by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **`-open-cmi` new compiler flag** to open a module signature directly from a `.cmi` file ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6013](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6013)) by [Jakub Staroń](https://github.com/staronj)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Fiber ID recording for magic-trace** via PTWRITE instruction when Intel PT is active ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6032](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6032)) by [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat)
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Localized `nondep_type_rec` memoisation state** ([ocaml/ocaml#14800](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14800)) by [Matthew Marques](https://github.com/mattm3465): new `Nondep_scope` module in `typing/ctype.ml` replaces module-global hash tables with per-call scope instances; reviewed by [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) and [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche)

-**Portable lock directories single solve** ([ocaml/dune#14458](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14458)): [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) opened a draft PR to implement a single SAT solve for all platforms, addressing [ocaml/dune#13645](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13645)
-**RPC debug improvements** ([ocaml/dune#14405](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14405)): [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) added in-flight RPC request tracking to SIGUSR2 debug dumps

-**Opam autopin cleanup UX** ([ocaml/opam#6923](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6923)): draft PR by [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) to silently unpin automatically pinned packages when the user declines installation confirmation

-**Opam repository load consistency** ([ocaml/opam#6924](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6924)): draft PR by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) to homogenise initial and diff-based repository loads, part of the tar.gz archive story ([ocaml/opam#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625))

-**odoc `[@@zero_alloc]` OxCaml annotation PR** ([ocaml/odoc#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)): by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV), parsing `zero_alloc` attributes with `opt`, `strict`, and `arity <n>` payloads; under active review by [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd)

-**odoc xref2 substitution bug fixes** ([ocaml/odoc#1430](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1430)): fix for assertion failure in `subst.ml` and uncaught exception in `apply_inner_substs` discovered via merlin-lib ([ocaml/odoc#1429](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1429)) and core_unix ([ocaml/odoc#1431](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1431)) build failures

-**js_of_ocaml variable coalescing fix** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2233](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2233)): corrected handling of unlabelled `break` targeting labelled non-iteration statements; CFG builder refactored with a new `visit_owning_stmt` to make context-ownership explicit

-**js_of_ocaml Wasm lifecycle events PR** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2232](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2232)): [Jason Gross](https://github.com/JasonGross) proposed dispatching `wasmoocaml:loaded` and `wasmoocaml:error` CustomEvents on `globalThis` when async Wasm instantiation completes

oxcaml: -**5.4 merge preparation** advanced via [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js)'s PRs for DWARF SLEB128 encoding ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6021](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6021)), frame pointer preservation at noalloc C calls ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6014](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6014)), and the staging integration PR ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5958](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5958))

-**x86 binary emitter correctness**: [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) opened NOP encoding/alignment ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6028](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6028), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6029](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6029)), monotone backwards jumps ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6025](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6025)), shorter 16-bit immediate arithmetic ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6027](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6027)), and removal of a jump distance heuristic ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6024](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6024))

-**Metaprogramming compilation unit unloading** PR opened by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5960](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5960)), introducing mark/sweep GC for unloadable CUs with code blocks as heap objects

-**SSA intermediate representation** PR opened by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5810](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5810)), a new backend pass between Cmm and CFG with a GADT-typed graph API and a `Cfg_compare` validator

-**Layout polymorphism** advances: `poly_` printing in value descriptions ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6038](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6038)) by [Andrej Ivašković](https://github.com/Dreian), layout-poly static data via callbacks ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6049](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6049)) and `Compilenv` split ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6030](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6030)) by [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr), persistent-module staticity via CMI modalities ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6041](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6041)) by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn)

-**Merlin build rules and import script** follow-ups after the subtree merge: [oxcaml/oxcaml#6042](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6042) and [oxcaml/oxcaml#6034](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6034) by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923)

-**`-open-cmi` new compiler flag** to open a module signature directly from a `.cmi` file ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6013](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6013)) by [Jakub Staroń](https://github.com/staronj)

-**Fiber ID recording for magic-trace** via PTWRITE instruction when Intel PT is active ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6032](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6032)) by [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat)
multicore: -[Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) opened [ocaml-multicore/eio#838](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/838) requesting an `Eio.Promise.map` function; [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) outlined three obstacles around promise resolution safety and lock concerns; [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) concluded his use case can accept a switch, resolving the immediate need

weekly_summary.cross_repository_work:
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Merlin module rename → ocaml-lsp compatibility**: [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923)'s relocation of `Misc` from `Merlin_utils` to `Ocaml_utils` in [ocaml/merlin#2059](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2059) required an immediate compatibility fix in [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1601](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1601) (merged by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos)) and an opam `pin-depends` fix in [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1603](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1603)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Merlin monorepo consolidation** links [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) and [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml): the standalone Merlin repo was deprecated ([oxcaml/merlin#240](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/240)) after its full history was merged as a git subtree ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5568](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5568)); two in-flight PRs ([oxcaml/merlin#223](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/223), [oxcaml/merlin#205](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/205)) were redirected to [oxcaml/oxcaml#6045](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6045) and [oxcaml/oxcaml#6044](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6044)
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **dune 3.23.0 regressions affecting downstream projects**: [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)'s dune 3.23 upgrade PR ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2231](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2231)) is blocked on [ocaml/dune#14441](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14441) (the 3.23.1 regression tracker) and [ocaml/dune#14438](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14438); [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) — the primary [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) maintainer — authored the fix for the jsoo shared runtime sort corruption directly in [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Relocatable compiler in dune connects to opam packaging**: [ocaml/dune#14357](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14357) (relocatable compiler support in `dune pkg`) relies on a relocatable OCaml compiler from David Allsopp's opam repository; [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) is also active in [ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) this week ([ocaml/odoc#1432](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1432)), adding an OxCaml opam conflict constraint
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **dune 3.23.0** was released to [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29844](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29844)) and simultaneously documented on [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3637](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3637)), with [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) authoring both the opam release and the website announcement
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **opam-repository dune 3.22.2+ox** ([oxcaml/opam-repository#39](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/39)) is a prerequisite for the latest odoc's OxCaml detection in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) CI — the upgrade was required to fix a CI breakage
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **[Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV)'s depext fixes** for conf-guile ([ocaml/opam-repository#29872](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29872)) were triggered by work on [ocaml/odoc#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422), illustrating how odoc development surfaces opam depext gaps across platforms
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Platform release documentation** in [ocaml/ocaml.org#3641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3641) covers releases for tools whose packages appear in opam-repository, including ocamlformat 0.29.0, odoc 3.2.0, merlin, ocaml-lsp, and opam-publish 3.0.0
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **OxCaml support coordinated across tools**: [ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc)'s OxCaml tracking issue ([ocaml/odoc#1411](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1411)) managed by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) — who also cut the dune 3.23.0 release ([ocaml/dune#14212](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14212)) — reflects a coordinated cross-project effort to align the toolchain with OxCaml compiler features
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **opam repository layer overhaul connects opam and dune package management**: the `OpamFilename.Unix` module introduced in [ocaml/opam#6915](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6915) and the `load_from_diff` consistency work ([ocaml/opam#6924](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6924)) are preparatory steps for tar.gz archive repository support ([ocaml/opam#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625)), which intersects with dune's package management layer

weekly_summary.notable_discussions:
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **dune 3.23.1 regression tracker** ([ocaml/dune#14441](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14441)): [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) opened a dedicated tracker immediately after 3.23.0. Identified regressions include menhir spurious dep injection ([ocaml/dune#14428](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14428)), opam bound deduplication activating for pre-3.23 projects ([ocaml/dune#14436](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14436)), OCaml < 4.14 build breakage ([ocaml/dune#14443](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14443)), and jsoo shared runtime ordering corruption ([ocaml/dune#14438](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14438))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Per-module inter-library dependency filtering** ([ocaml/dune#14472](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14472)): [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) restructured the long-running [ocaml/dune#14116](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14116) PR; [Alain Frisch](https://github.com/alainfrisch) engaged on the trade-off between per-module fine-grained deps versus compiler-side `.cmx` stability checks
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **ocamlgrep integration architecture** ([ocaml/merlin#2063](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2063)): [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) proposed integrating structural type-aware OCaml code search as a Merlin subcommand ([ocaml/merlin#2064](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2064)); [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) raised concerns about dependency layering; the discussion also surfaced the project's position on AI-assisted contributions
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **ocaml-lsp opam downgrade on OCaml 4.14** ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1604](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1604)): [Török Edwin](https://github.com/edwintorok) reported that `opam upgrade` attempts to downgrade `ocaml-lsp-server` from `1.21.0-4.14` to `1.17.0` due to version resolution interplay with the `lsp` sub-library split
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **odoc `[@@zero_alloc]` representation debate** ([ocaml/odoc#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)): [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) and [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) debated a record type `{ opt; strict; arity }` vs. a variant representation, and whether to silently ignore invalid payloads
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Mode solver `Map` vs `Hashtbl` performance** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5962](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5962)): [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) and [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) discussed correctness and performance trade-offs noting a 3% regression; [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) opened a separate fix using a total structural order for solver morph map keys ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6047](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6047))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **`borrow_` aliasing documentation** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5928](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5928)): [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) and [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) debated whether `borrow_` aliasing behavior should be documented given it is an implementation artifact rather than a designed feature
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Runtime event flooding in large Infer runs** ([ocaml/ocaml#14791](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14791)) opened by [nicki](https://github.com/curche): proposes emitting `EV_DOMAIN_CONDITION_WAIT` only in the instrumented runtime after observing 46K events vs. ~750 for other phases at `-j14`; [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) questioned whether the event should be removed entirely
- __RUMINANT:core__ **`Dynarray` concurrency documentation precision** ([ocaml/ocaml#14798](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14798)) by [Nathan Taylor](https://github.com/dijkstracula): [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) confirmed only resizing operations are truly unsafe, noting broader work on documenting concurrent usage for all stdlib mutable structures remains pending
- __RUMINANT:core__ **`Nonempty_list.t` type for stdlib** ([ocaml/ocaml#14261](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/discussions/14261)) by [Mathieu Barbin](https://github.com/mbarbin): ongoing discussion on adding a non-empty list type using the `(::)` constructor trick for list-syntax compatibility, purely for interoperability between third-party libraries
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Release documentation process concerns** ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3641)): [Jules Aguillon](https://github.com/Julow) flagged malformed code blocks in draft mdx 2.5.2 changelog; [@sabine](https://github.com/sabine) raised a process concern that release announcements should be written by maintainers on discuss.ocaml.org first
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **ocaml-src dev package archival** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29881](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29881)): [Reynir Björnsson](https://github.com/reynir) proposed archiving `ocaml-src.{4.13,4.14,5.0}.dev` packages that have no checksums and are marked `avoid-version`; [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) agreed they appear unused since OCaml 5.1
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **`Eio.Promise.map` design constraints** ([ocaml-multicore/eio#838](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/838)): [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) explained that `Condition.register_immediate` exists for immediate callbacks but is intended for IO library integration rather than general use, clarifying the tension between ergonomic functional composition and Eio's deliberate constraint that promise resolution must not execute arbitrary user code

- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Binary emitter systematic review by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon)**: three VEX encoding PRs landed and five more opened in one week, indicating a methodical audit of x86 emitter correctness covering NOP padding, jump encoding, and prefix selection ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6012](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6012), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6022](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6022), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6033](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6033), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6028](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6028), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6029](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6029))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Layout polymorphism advancing on multiple fronts simultaneously**: `val poly_` printing ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6038](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6038)), layout variables in product kinds ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6026](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6026)), `Compilenv` split ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6030](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6030)), static data via callbacks ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6049](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6049)), and foundational `any`-kind PRs ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5461](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5461), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5462](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5462))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **The OCaml compiler floor is rising**: the archiving policy cutoff was updated from 4.08 to 4.11 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29848](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29848)), and dune 3.23.0 itself raised its minimum build requirement from 4.08 to 4.14 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29844](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29844)); a further cleanup of ocaml-src dev packages without checksums is under discussion ([ocaml/opam-repository#29881](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29881))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **The robur-coop Miou ecosystem** produced coordinated releases across nine packages (miou, mkernel, mnet, mtbox, mhttp, mfat, hurl, httpcats, vif) in a single week, reflecting active development of a unikernel networking stack built on the Miou scheduler and extending the trend from week 18's terminal UI and pooling additions
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Platform release documentation catching up**: multiple platform tool releases from February–May 2026 had no corresponding ocaml.org announcement pages; [ocaml/ocaml.org#3641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3641) represents an effort to retroactively document these, while dune alpha changelogs are now tracked with `ignore: true` ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3640](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3640))
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Removal of global mutable state from the typechecker** continues: [ocaml/ocaml#14800](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14800) (`Nondep_scope`) and [ocaml/ocaml#14795](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14795) convert module-level hash tables and dead mutual recursions into locally-scoped values, improving thread-safety and modularity of compiler internals
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Draft GC pacing algorithm** ([ocaml/ocaml#14796](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14796)) by [Damien Doligez](https://github.com/damiendoligez) introduces a new GC pacing algorithm (686 additions across 17 files) as an early-stage draft, signalling upcoming changes to GC throughput tuning
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Build engine resource management in dune**: multiple merged PRs this week ([ocaml/dune#14464](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14464), [ocaml/dune#14483](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14483), [ocaml/dune#14487](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14487)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) reflect a sustained drive to reduce memory pressure, file descriptor exhaustion, and disk usage in long-running watch sessions
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Post-release regression discipline in dune**: the 3.23.1 cycle ([ocaml/dune#14441](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14441)) demonstrates a maturing practice of gating behaviour changes on `(lang dune X.Y)` version, with [ocaml/dune#14466](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14466) and [ocaml/dune#14453](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14453) retroactively applying version guards to previously unguarded changes
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Upstream patch absorption in oxcaml/opam-repository**: removal of odoc 3.1.0+ox packages ([oxcaml/opam-repository#43](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/43)) and closure of the odoc-parser compatibility PR ([oxcaml/opam-repository#22](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/22)) reflect a pattern where OxCaml-specific patches are upstreamed, allowing the overlay to shift from custom patches to plain version constraints
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Windows-specific OCaml packages growing**: sha256-cng 0.1.0 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29885](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29885)) uses the native Windows CNG/BCrypt API, and ocaml_intrinsics_kernel v0.17.2 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29880](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29880)) fixed MSVC compilation errors, continuing a pattern of increased Windows ecosystem investment alongside the core compiler's cross-platform Unix fixes this week
